Titulo: Differences in event-related potential (ERP) responses to small tie, non-tie and 1-problems in addition and multiplication
Autores: Jeanne Bagnoud, Jasinta Dewi, Catherine Thevenot
Amostra_Info: 25 students (ages 18–24; mean age 21.24). Healthy adults (3 left-handed and 8 excluded due to EEG recording errors).
Marcadores_Amostra: students
Processos_Matematicos: One-digit addition and multiplication problems with operands from 1 to 5. Categories: Tie problems, Nontie problems, 1problems.
Comparacao_Tarefa: different difficulty levels within the task itself (the authors do not compare the mathematical task with a non-mathematical task).
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy. Solution times. Reported retrieval rate (%).
Posicao_Eletrodos: anterior frontal, frontal, parietal, occipital
Equipamento: Electroencephalography (EEG) with Biosemi ActiveTwo system. QWERTZ keyboard.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 electrodes (10-20 layout). Electrodes grouped into 9 areas and 3 lateral zones (left, right, midline).
Pre_Processamento: (revisar pre-processamento)
Faixas_Frequencia: The study did not explicitly analyze canonical frequency bands (e.g., alpha, beta). Instead, filtering was applied using a 50 Hz notch filter, a 0.01 Hz high-pass filter, and a 60 Hz low-pass filter.
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA, Greenhouse-Geisser correction, Holm correction, Bayesian t-tests.

Titulo: EEG correlates of math anxiety during arithmetic problem solving: Implication for attention deficits
Autores: Jie Liu, Jinqi Li, Weiwei Peng, Mengjiao Feng, Yuejia Luo
Amostra_Info: 74 right-handed university students (HMA: 19.88 y; LMA: 19.53 y)
Marcadores_Amostra: students, math anxiety
Processos_Matematicos: Division and mod problems
Comparacao_Tarefa: anticipation period before a problem appears
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy and RT (outliers removed).
Posicao_Eletrodos: anterior frontal, frontal, parietal, occipital
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 Ag/AgCl scalp electrodes (10–20 system).
Pre_Processamento: Epoching (−1000 to +1000 ms), baseline correction
Faixas_Frequencia: Beta (18–26 Hz) Gamma (31–40 Hz and 32–45 Hz). Lower bands were also included in the analysis (1-8 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Two-way mixed ANOVA, point-by-point two-way mixed ANOVA with permutation testing, hierarchical linear regression, and post-hoc t-tests.

Titulo: EEG coherence in a mental arithmetic task performance in first episode schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorder
Autores: Yuliya Zaytseva, Zhanna Garakh, Vladimir NovototskyVlasov, Isaac Ya. Gurovich, Alexander Shmukler, Anna Papaefstathiou, Jiří Horáček, Filip Španiel, Valeria B. Strelets
Amostra_Info: 32 SZ, 32 SA, 40 HC. Matched for gender, age, education.
Marcadores_Amostra: schizophrenia or schizoaffective disorder
Processos_Matematicos: serial subtraction (Kraepelin counting, −7 from 200)
Comparacao_Tarefa: resting state baseline
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Correct answers, counting speed.
Posicao_Eletrodos: Frontal/posterior, inter/intrahemispheric, midline.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 electrodes (10–20 system). 4 participants had 16.
Pre_Processamento: low-pass filter at 70 Hz and a time constant of 0.3s
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta: 1.5-3.9 Hz Theta: 4-7 Hz Alpha: 8-13 Hz Beta1: 13-20 Hz Beta2: 20-30 Hz Gamma: 30-40 Hz
Software_Analise: STATISTICA 10.0custom-designed software was used for coherence analysis and preprocessing, with the help of an EEG mapper (MBM, Russia)
Abordagem_Estatistica: t-test, repeated measures ANOVA, one-way ANOVA, two-way ANOVA, post-hoc comparisons with Bonferroni correction, Pearson correlation with FDR correction.

Titulo: Visualization of Human Cognitive States Monitored by Highdensity EEG Arrays
Autores: J. J. J. Davis, R. Kozma
Amostra_Info: 3 adults (2 advanced meditators, 1 novice).
Marcadores_Amostra: meditators
Processos_Matematicos: Arithmetic processing after verbal prompt (MathMind)
Comparacao_Tarefa: meditative state with eyes closed and a state of open eyes with minimal blinking
Variaveis_Fisiologicas: HRV, sleep monitoring, pulse and BP pre-experiment.
Posicao_Eletrodos: Full scalp/face..
Equipamento: HydroCel Geodesic Sensor Net (HCGSN) by Electrical Geodesics, Inc. (EGI), 256 channels. Other: HeartMath emWave Pro (for HRV) and a Sleep Tracker.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 256 electrodes (16×16 matrix)., HydroCel Geodesic Sensor Net (HCGSN)
Pre_Processamento: (revisar pre-processamento)
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta: 4-6 Hz Alpha: 8-12 Hz Low Beta: 14-18 Hz High Beta: 20-26 Hz Low Gamma: 28-36 Hz High Gamma: 38-48 Hz
Abordagem_Estatistica: Comparative statistical analysis between modalities; Mean Frequency (MFAC(t)) calculation; Mean of differences in frequency between modalities (MDIFF(t)) calculation; Boxplots; Euclidean Distance (ED) calculation; Mean and 95% confidence intervals calculation

Titulo: Power Spectral Density Changes in the EEG during Mental Arithmetic and Eye-Opening
Amostra_Info: 15 subjects, with 11 subjects in the complete experiment
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic, specifically solving two-digit multiplication problems
Comparacao_Tarefa: "eyes closed" resting condition and the "eyes open" condition
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Performance
Posicao_Eletrodos: bipolar electrodes placement: C3-P3, P3-O1, C4-P4, P4-O2
Equipamento: Ampex Portable Instrumentation as equipament of eeg and a Oscilloscope
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 4 bipolar electrodes made of silver
Pre_Processamento: (revisar pre-processamento)
Faixas_Frequencia: The frequencies analyzed were from 0 to 30 Hz, in increments of 2.5 Hz. (Delta, Alfa and Beta)
Software_Analise: Noratom Instrument for Statistical Analysis and Computation (ISAC)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Calculation of Ratios using Comparison "t" tests.

Titulo: A tensorial approach to access cognitive worload related to mental arithmetic from eeg functional connectivity estimates
Autores: S.I. Dimitriadis, Yu Sun, K. Kwok, N.A. Laskaris, A. Bezerianos*
Revista: 35th Annual International Conference of the IEEE EMBS
Amostra_Info: 1 Single participant (male).
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic task (addition)
Comparacao_Tarefa: different difficulty levels within the task itself (the authors do not compare the mathematical task with a non-mathematical task).
Variaveis_Comportamentais: reaction time and accuracy (in terms of correct or incorrect responses).
Posicao_Eletrodos: Parieto-occipital regions.
Equipamento: An ActiveTwo Biosemi system was used.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 channels
Pre_Processamento: PLV – Phase Locking Value, Local Efficiency (LE), Reduced Subgraph Connections and Low-Dimensional Subspace Representation (TSA output)
Faixas_Frequencia: Signals were band-pass filtered from 0.5 to 45 Hz, covering delta, theta, alpha, beta, and gamma bands.
Abordagem_Estatistica: Comparison of Local Efficiency and Classifier Validation with cross-validation scheme
Classificacao_ML: TSA, LDA, kNN

Titulo: Building Networks with a New Cross-Bubble Transition Entropy for Quantitative Assessment of Mental Arithmetic Electroencephalogram
Autores: Xiaobi Chen, Guanghua Xu, Sicong Zhang , Xun Zhang and Zhicheng Teng
Amostra_Info: 36 subjects (9 men and 27 women, aged between 16 and 26 years), whose EEG data met the requirements for analysis after visual inspection. One subject (subject 31) was excluded because the length of their recordings was different from the others.
Processos_Matematicos: subtraction (mental arithmetic)
Comparacao_Tarefa: resting state
Variaveis_Comportamentais: cognitive load
Posicao_Eletrodos: prefrontal and frontal lobes (FP1, FP2, F3, Fz, F4, F7, and F8)
Equipamento: Neurocom monopolar EEG 23-channel system (from Ukraine, XAI-MEDICA)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 7 channels, silver/silver chloride electrodes.
Pre_Processamento: Average Clustering Coefficients - ACE and Global Network Efficiency - GNE
Faixas_Frequencia: 1 to 42 Hz - delta, teta, alfa, beta and low-gama
Abordagem_Estatistica: Nonparametric Permutation Test (Individual Subject Level) and Paired Sample t-test (Group Level)
Classificacao_ML: CBTN - Cross-Bubble Transition Network

Titulo: Inter-brain coupling reflects disciplinary differences in real-world classroom learning
Autores: Jingjing Chen, Penghao Qian, Xinqiao Gao, Baosong Li, Yu Zhang and Dan Zhang
Amostra_Info: 34 Grade 10 students
Marcadores_Amostra: high school students
Processos_Matematicos: learning mathematics
Comparacao_Tarefa: different academic discipline (Chinese)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: final exam scores
Posicao_Eletrodos: frontal lobe: Fp1 and Fp2
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 2 channels, dry
Pre_Processamento: Total Interdependence (TI) Method, Student-Class Coupling, Student-Top Coupling
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (4-8 Hz) Alpha (8-13 Hz) Low-beta (13-18 Hz) High-beta (18-30 Hz)
Software_Analise: Matlab language, NoiseTools toolbox, MSDL toolbox
Abordagem_Estatistica: Pearson's correlations and nonparametric permutation tests (5000 permutations)
Notas: deixaram link com os códigos desenvolvidos

Titulo: Cognitive Stress Recognition during Mathematical Task and EEG changes following Audio-Visual Stimuli for Relaxation
Autores: Rashmi C R, Dr Shantala C P
Amostra_Info: 21 to 25 years.
Processos_Matematicos: arithmetic puzzles.
Comparacao_Tarefa: non-numeric verbal/perceptual control state, specifically a "happy state" induced by watching funny video clips.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: stress and happiness
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 8-channels
Pre_Processamento: Parks-McClellan optimal finite impulse response (FIR) filter with a frequency range of 2–40 Hz., Relative Band Power
Abordagem_Estatistica: The primary statistical approach was a comparison of EEG relative band power between the two mental states, presented using error bars.
Classificacao_ML: Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) deep learning model.

Titulo: EEG Estimates of Engagement and Cognitive Workload Predict Math Problem Solving Outcomes
Autores: Federico Cirett Gal´an and Carole R. Beal
Amostra_Info: 16 students (8 males, 8 females), college students who were at least 18 years old
Marcadores_Amostra: college students
Processos_Matematicos: SAT items
Comparacao_Tarefa: resting baseline, different difficulty levels
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy, Reaction time, Self-assessments (difficulty, confidence, and frustration on a five-point Likert scale).
Posicao_Eletrodos: prefrontal and frontal lobes, with two reference signals attached to the mastoid bones (behind the ears) and two sensors attached to the right clavicle and to the lowest left rib to record the heart rate
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 9 Channels
Pre_Processamento: Engagement/Workload Signal Binning, Transition Probability Table (TPT), Problem Outcome Tagging.
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta, Alpha, Beta and Sigma wave signals (ranging from 3 Hz to 40 Hz) Note: The frequency ranges are listed as 3-40 Hz for all bands, which is a very broad range and may be a misstatement in the abstract.
Abordagem_Estatistica: t-tests (matched-pairs), Correlation analysis, Principal components analysis (PCA)
Classificacao_ML: Support Vector Machine (SVM)

Titulo: Optimized gamma synchronization enhances functional binding of fronto-parietal cortices in mathematically gifted adolescents during deductive reasoning
Autores: Li Zhang, John Q. Gan, and HaixianWang
Amostra_Info: 24 participants. Math-gifted group: 11 adolescents (8 males, 3 females), ages 15–18 years old (mean age 16.3 ± 0.6). Control group: 13 adolescents (8 males, 5 females), ages 15–18 years old (mean age 15.9 ± 0.7).
Marcadores_Amostra: math-gifted, adolescents from a specific school
Processos_Matematicos: deductive reasoning, categorical syllogism task of analytic type (verbal-logical way).
Comparacao_Tarefa: baseline resting state and a baseline block of trials that did not require reasoning.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: accuracy, reaction time
Posicao_Eletrodos: frontal and parietal cortices. bilateral mastoids
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 60 scalp electrodes
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtering, ERS/ERD, Brainstorm Toolbox, SLORETA, PLV, adjacency matrices, modularity, characteristic path length, clustering coefficient, betweenness centrality, Phase-Locking Intervals (PLI), power-law fitting.
Faixas_Frequencia: gamma-band (30–60 Hz)
Software_Analise: Matlab language, Matlab Statistics Toolbox, EEGLAB, Brainstorm Toolbox, OpenMEEG. Scan 4.3, Brainstorm, and Matlab.
Abordagem_Estatistica: One-way ANOVA, Bonferroni Corrections
Classificacao_ML: LDA – Linear Discriminant Analysis

Titulo: Oscillatory EEG correlates of arithmetic strategies: a training study
Autores: Roland H. Grabner and Bert De Smedt
Amostra_Info: 20 adult students, 9 males and 11 females, Ranged between 21 and 32 years ol
Marcadores_Amostra: adult students
Processos_Matematicos: arithmetic strategies, fact retrieval for solving arithmetic problems, specifically two-digit multiplication problems.
Comparacao_Tarefa: non-arithmetic figural-spatial task and to a pre-stimulus reference interval (fixation period)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Self-reported Strategy Use, Accuracy and time
Posicao_Eletrodos: full 10–20 systems
Equipamento: BioSemi ActiveTwo system and eog equipament
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 channels
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtering (0.5–45 Hz), EOG artifact reduction (regression), 3D spline Laplacian, FFT-based FIR filtering, moving window power calculation, ERS/ERD in theta and alpha bands, % change from baseline, cortical area aggregation.
Faixas_Frequencia: theta (3-6 Hz), lower alpha (8-10 Hz), and upper alpha (10-13 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA, Huynh–Feldt procedure, Effect size measures, t-tests

Titulo: CLASSIFICATION OF VERBAL AND MATHEMATICAL MENTAL OPERATIONS BASED ON THE POWER SPECTRAL DENSITY OF EEG
Autores: E.V. CHEMERISOVA, M.S. ATANOV, I.N. MIKHEEV, O.V. MARTYNOVA
Revista: Psychology. Journal of the Higher School of Economics.
Amostra_Info: 19 healthy right-handed volunteers participated in the study. All subjects were students or alumni of higher education institutions in Moscow. They were divided into two groups.
Marcadores_Amostra: students or alumni of higher education institutions. educational background, which was either math or humanities
Processos_Matematicos: Simple arithmetic operations: addition, subtraction, fraction, or multiplication. Logical tasks with arithmetic progressions: extending sequences of integers.
Comparacao_Tarefa: verbal tasks (solving anagrams) and to each other (arithmetic vs. logical tasks).
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Decision Time (DT), task accuracy, false answers (FA), and the number of unresolved tasks (UT)..
Posicao_Eletrodos: 10-20 system
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 21 channels
Pre_Processamento: filtered in the 1-40 Hz band. Eye movement artifacts were removed by linear regression using EOG signals. Muscle and other artifacts were manually cleaned. Segmented into epochs. Absolute values of the power spectral density (PSD) from Fast Fourier Transformation (FFT)
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA, Tukey post-hoc comparison, Mann-Whitney U-test, Spearman's rank correlation analysis
Classificacao_ML: ANN (Perceptron without hidden layers)

Titulo: Theta power reduction and theta–gamma coupling desynchronization are associated with working memory interference and anxiety symptoms in panic disorder: a retrospective study
Autores: Ji Seon Ahn, Hye‐Jin Hong, Jee Hang Lee, and Jin Young Park
Amostra_Info: 68 participants were included in the final analysis, divided into two groups: Panic Disorder (PD) Group: 34 patients diagnosed with PD by experienced psychiatrists using the criteria of the Structured Clinical Interview for the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, 5th edition. Healthy Control (HC) Group: 34 age- and sex-matched healthy controls.
Marcadores_Amostra: Panic Disorder (PD)
Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic (MA)
Comparacao_Tarefa: 5-minute eyes-closed resting-state (RS) condition.
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Hamilton Anxiety Scale (HAMA), Hamilton Rating Scale for Depression (HAMD), State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI-S), Perceived Stress Scale (PSS)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: average accuracy
Posicao_Eletrodos: Frontal: AF4, F2, FCz, Fp2, Fz, FC1, AFz, F1, FP1, AF3, F3, F5, FC5, FC3, F7, FT7, FC4, FC2, FT8, FC6, F8, F6, F4 Temporal: T3, TP7, T5, T4, T6, TP8 Central: C1, C3, CP1, C5, C2, CP2, C4, C6, Cz Parietal: P5, P3, P1, P9, PO3, Pz, POz, PO4, P2, P4, P6 Occipital: O1, Oz, O2 Other: A1, A2
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 57 recording electrodes. The electrodes used were sponge-based carbon fiber electrodes (Ag/AgCl-coated and carbon-filled plastic electrodes with a sponge).
Pre_Processamento: Theta-gamma coupling (TGC)
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (4-8 Hz) Gamma (30-50 Hz)
Software_Analise: Matlab language, EEGLAB toolbox, SPSS version 25.0
Abordagem_Estatistica: Descriptive Statistics, Normality Test, Comparative Analyses between PD patients and HCs, Multiple Comparisons Adjustment, Correlation Analysis, Linear Regression Models

Titulo: Electrophysiological Brain Response to Error in Solving Mathematical Tasks
Autores: Francisco J. Alvarado-Rodríguez, Karla P. Ibarra-González, Cristina Eccius-Wellmann,Hugo Vélez-Pérez and Rebeca Romo-Vázquez
Amostra_Info: 20 healthy engineering students (13 young men, 7 young women)
Marcadores_Amostra: engineering students who scored in the average range (Grade III) on the Raven's Progressive Matrices Test.
Processos_Matematicos: arithmetic and algebraic operations
Comparacao_Tarefa: resting baseline
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Response Time, acurracy
Posicao_Eletrodos: center-medial region of the brain. Fp1/2, F3/4, F7/8, C3/4, P3/4, T3/4, T5/6, O1/2, Fz, Cz e Pz
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 electrodes
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtering (0.5–45 Hz), Blind Source Separation (artifact reduction), 3D spline Laplacian, FFT power spectrum, 1-second windowing (V1–V4), %ERS/ERD in theta and alpha bands, relative energy of delta, theta, alpha, beta bands, cortical area aggregation.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta (1-3 Hz) Theta (4-7 Hz) Alpha (8-13 Hz) Beta (14-30 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Descriptive analysis, Wilcoxon rank-sum test, two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test.

Titulo: Arithmetic Mental Task of EEG Signal Classification Using Statistical Modeling of The DWT Coefficient
Autores: Sk. Md. Ziad Rahman, Israt Tawana, H.M. Raihan Mostafiz, Tanima Tasmin Chowdhury, Celia Shahnaz
Revista: International Conference on Computer, Communication, Chemical, Materials and Electronic Engineering
Amostra_Info: 36 healthy subjects
Marcadores_Amostra: Bad-counters (n=10):hose who finished the task with a high level of difficulty and required excessive effort. Good-counters (n=26): Those who finished the task with no difficulty.
Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic tasks, specifically serial subtractions of two numbers.
Comparacao_Tarefa: resting state
Variaveis_Comportamentais: level of difficulty/effort
Posicao_Eletrodos: Anterior Frontal: Fp1, Fp2 Frontal: F3, F4, Fz, F7, F8 Central: C3, C4, Cz Parietal: P3, P4, Pz Occipital: O1, O2 Temporal: T3, T4, T5, T6
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 23 channels (electrodes). Silver or silver chloride (Ag/AgCl) electrodes
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtering (0.5–45 Hz), notch filter (50 Hz), DWT using Haar wavelet, data augmentation, dominant channel selection. DWT coefficients and statistical parameters from Gaussian modeling of the coefficients.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta (1-3 Hz) Theta (4-7 Hz) Alpha (8-13 Hz) Beta (14-30 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Gaussian modeling of DWT coefficients, Bhattacharyya Distance, Geometrical Separability Index (GSI).
Classificacao_ML: SVM, kNN

Titulo: Individual Differences in Math Ability Determine Neurocognitive Processing of Arithmetic Complexity: A Combined fNIRS-EEG Study
Autores: Christina Artemenko, Mojtaba Soltanlou, Silke M. Bieck, Ann-Christine Ehlis, Thomas Dresler and Hans-Christoph Nuerk
Amostra_Info: 34 subjects. High Math Ability Group: 17 participants. Low Math Ability Group: 17 participants
Marcadores_Amostra: mathematical ability
Processos_Matematicos: multiplication and division
Comparacao_Tarefa: inter-trial interval baseline of 4–7 seconds.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Response time (RT), Error rates (ER), Strategy use (via self-report, categorized as arithmetic fact retrieval, counting, decomposition, etc.).
Posicao_Eletrodos: Frontal: FP1, Fz, FP2, AFF7h (instead of F7), AFF3 (instead of F3), Fz, AFF4 (instead of F4), AFF8h (instead of F8) Central: FCC3 (instead of C3), Cz, FCC4 (instead of C4) Temporal: T7, T8 Parietal: TPP7h (instead of P7), CPP3 (instead of P3), Pz, CPP4 (instead of P4), TPP8h (instead of P8) Occipital: O1, Oz, O2
Equipamento: fNIRS-EEG cap (Brain Products GmbH, Herrsching, Germany) and ETG-4000 Optical Topography System
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 21 scalp electrodes
Pre_Processamento: fNIRS preprocessing: Bandpass filter (0.02–0.25 Hz).Correlation-based signal improvement (CBSI). General linear model approach to get beta values.EEG preprocessing:Re-referenced to average reference. Bandpass filter (0.1–40 Hz). Signal space projections to remove eye movement artifacts.Power spectral density (PSD) for different frequency bands.Event-related synchronization (ERS) and event-related desynchronization (ERD) percentage values.
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (4-7 Hz) Lower Alpha (8-10 Hz) Upper Alpha (10-13 Hz)
Software_Analise: Matlab language Matlab, Brainstorm. SPSS (IBM SPSS Statistics). Presentation software (NeuroBehavioral Systems, Inc.).
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVAs, one-sample t-test, paired t-test, independent two-sample t-test, Dubey/Armitage-Parmar correction, Bonferroni correction.

Titulo: Automatic detection of mental arithmetic task and its difficulty level using EEG signals
Autores: Binish Fatimah, Dipro Pramanick and Pranav Shivashankaran
Revista: 11th International Conference on Computing, Communication and Networking Technologies (ICCCNT)
Amostra_Info: 36 subjects
Marcadores_Amostra: university students, accuracy on a mental arithmetic task
Processos_Matematicos: serial subtractions of two numbers
Comparacao_Tarefa: rest state, different difficulty levels within the task itself
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Number of correct arithmetic operations completed per minute.
Posicao_Eletrodos: Anterior Frontal: Fp1, Fp2 Frontal: F3, F4, Fz, F7, F8 Central: C3, C4, Cz Parietal: P3, P4, Pz Occipital: O1, O2 Temporal: T3, T4, T5, T6
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 23 channels silver or silver chloride (Ag/AgCl) electrodes
Pre_Processamento: High-pass filter (cutoff frequency 0.5 Hz). Low-pass filter (cutoff frequency 45 Hz). Power-line interference removal filter. Energy, Entropy, Mean, and L2 norm calculated for each EEG sub-band rhythm (gamma, beta, alpha, theta, delta); decomposing EEG signals into these five sub-band rhythms using a filter bank.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta: less than 4 Hz Theta: 4-8 Hz Alpha: 8-13 Hz Beta: 13-30 Hz Gamma: above 30 Hz
Abordagem_Estatistica: Descriptive analysis for performance and task difficulty;
Classificacao_ML: Support Vector Machines (SVM); Decision Tree; Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA)

Titulo: Mining EEG with SVM for Understanding Cognitive Underpinnings of Math Problem Solving Strategies
Autores: Paul Bosch, Mauricio Herrera, Julio López and Sebastián Maldonado
Amostra_Info: 14 participants
Marcadores_Amostra: first-year engineering students
Processos_Matematicos: arithmetic and algebraic operations (attribute substitution, intuitive rules, and conceptual metaphors)
Comparacao_Tarefa: baseline period of 500 ms before the problem is presented. The main analysis, however, is a direct comparison between brain patterns associated with correct versus incorrect answers.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Response Time, acurracy
Posicao_Eletrodos: Parietal structures Temporal structures Frontal structures
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 61 sensors (electrodes)
Pre_Processamento: Filtered (FIR, band-pass of 1–100 Hz). Re-referenced against the common average reference. Segmented into 1-second epochs. Subtracted the average EEG-evoked activity from the total EEG activity.Correlations between EEG channels, Phase Locking Value (PLV), feature vectors from correlation and synchronization matrices, and most relevant network connections identified by feature selection.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta (<4 Hz)Theta (4−8 Hz). Alpha (8−12 Hz) Beta (14−30 Hz). Gamma (30−80 Hz).
Software_Analise: Matlab language, Matlab Toolbox: SeDuMi for ξ-SOCP. Matlab Toolbox: LibSVM for standard SVM. Matlab, NS3 software
Abordagem_Estatistica: Fisher score and RFE-SVM for feature selection; nested 10-fold cross-validation for model evaluation.
Classificacao_ML: Soft-Margin SVM, Twin SVM (TB-SVM), and ξ-Second-Order Cone Programming SVM (ξ-SOCP)
Notas: The paper mentions using code provided by the author of Twin-Bounded SVM.

Titulo: Continuous decoding of cognitive load from electroencephalography reveals task-general and task-specific correlates
Autores: Matthew J Boring, Karl Ridgeway, Michael Shvartsman and Tanya R Jonker
Amostra_Info: 33 participants
Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic, specifically serial summation of three 3-digit numbers.
Comparacao_Tarefa: low-load mental arithmetic condition (adding only the leftmost digit of each number) and to two other tasks: an n-back task and a multi-object tracking (MOT) task. The study also uses a 5-minute resting phase
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Self-reported mental effort, Response times (RT), Accuracy
Posicao_Eletrodos: specifically mentioning parietal alpha and frontal beta activity
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 channels active Ag/AgCl electrodes
Pre_Processamento: EEG preprocessing: re-referencing, bandpass filtering, down-sampling, and artifact removal (ASR). Power Spectral Density (PSD) estimated using Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) with sliding windows. Extraction of spectral features (PSD values) over time and channels.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta/Theta (1-7 Hz) Alpha (8-12 Hz) Beta (13-30 Hz) Gamma (31-45 Hz)
Software_Analise: MATLAB language, EEGLAB toolbox, LIBSVM toolbox, R (for statistical analysis)
Abordagem_Estatistica: mixed-effects models for behavioral data and SVM performance, ROC analysis to obtain AUC values for SVM classifiers, and parametric bootstrap to compute 95% confidence intervals for model coefficients.
Classificacao_ML: SVMs, k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN), Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA).

Titulo: FREQUENCY AND POWER ANALYSIS OF EEG THETA ACTIVITY DURING DIFFERENT NUMERICAL AND VERBAL TASK IN MALES
Autores: Mehmet Cihan
Revista: International Journal of Health Services Research and Policy
Amostra_Info: 20 healthy medical male students without any health problems were included in the study. average age is 21.15±2.10 years.
Marcadores_Amostra: medical male students
Processos_Matematicos: numerical operations: multiplication, division, addition and subtraction
Comparacao_Tarefa: verbal task (answering general knowledge questions) and to the same task with incorrect answers.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: measure the brain response to seeing a correct vs. an incorrect answer
Posicao_Eletrodos: only mention the left side fo the brain
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 3 electrodes
Pre_Processamento: Domain measurements: Minimum amplitude value (Min), Maximum amplitude value (Max), Mean aplitude value (Mean), Standard deviation (Std), Skew, Kurtosis and Linear regression (Lin reg). In adicionnal, the Fast Fourier Transformation (FFT) and the Power Spectral Density (PSD) were used.
Faixas_Frequencia: Only the theta wave frequency band was analyzed in this study.
Software_Analise: - Biopac MP36 Student Lab software version 4.1, Statistical Package for Social Sciences version 21.0 (SPSS)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Descriptive statistics (Mean ± SD, Median)., Normality tests (Kolmogorov-Smirnov and Shapiro-Wilk), Paired t-test and Wilcoxon matched-pair test

Titulo: Can Music Support Calculation Skills? A Pilot Study Using Electrophysiological Measures
Autores: Nurdan Korkmaza,Özlem Doğan Temurb
Amostra_Info: 4 estudantes voluntários com idade entre 9~11 anos diagnosticados com MLD( Dificuldades de aprendizagem em matemática de nivel leve(20%)
Marcadores_Amostra: Dificuldade de Aprendizagem em Matemática (DAM), alunos da terceira e quarta classes, com idades entre 9 e 11 anos
Processos_Matematicos: Cálculo mental , especificamente adição e subtração
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Acurácia (número de respostas corretas): O total de acertos dos quatro participantes aumentou de 44 no pré-teste para 83 no pós-teste, um aumento descrito no estudo como significativo.
Posicao_Eletrodos: Diferentes partes do escalpo(Montagem nao explicita)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 16 eletrodos, montagem não mencionada no artigo
Pre_Processamento: Cálculo da Densidade de Potência Espectral através do metodo de Welch p/ alfa,beta,theta,delta , uma diminuição na potência Alfa e um aumento na potência Beta foi interpreada como indicativo de maior atividade mental e atenção. O artigo não especifica unidades de medida de potência nem outras métricas.
Faixas_Frequencia: Análise de frequência na faixa de 1 a 48 Hz. Cálculo da densidade de potência para as bandas Alfa, Beta, Teta e Delta utilizando o método de Welch.
Software_Analise: Para a análise do sinal de EEG, o softwareMATLAB. A análise estatística dos dados foi conduzida por meio de uma abordagem descritiva, não especifica software para estatística
Classificacao_ML: Análise da Densidade Espectral de Potência (PSD) através do método de Welch. Análise estatística realizada por meio de técnicas descritivas, com comparação dos totais de acertos pré e pós-intervenção.

Titulo: Transition dynamics of EEG-based network microstates during mental arithmetic and resting wakefulness reflects task-related modulations and developmental changes
Autores: S. I. Dimitriadis • N. A. Laskaris • S. Micheloyannis
Amostra_Info: 20 children(8-12 years) and 25 students(21-26 years)
Marcadores_Amostra: estudantes
Processos_Matematicos: Arithmetic comparisons, 2 digits for children, 3 digits for adults
Comparacao_Tarefa: estado de repouso
Variaveis_Psicologicas: WISC-III test, Illinois test of psycholinguistic abilities. ZAREKI test, child behavior checklist
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Performance on arithmetic problems: This variable was indeed measured. The study included an "arithmetic comparison task" , and the article notes that "The performance of subjects, in the comparison task was very high (100%)".
Posicao_Eletrodos: international 10/20 system (FP2,F4, FC4, C4, CP4, P4, O2, F8, FT8, T4, TP8, PO8, Fz,FCz, Cz, CPz, Pz, Oz, FP1, F3, FC3, C3, CP3, P3, O1, F7,FT7, T3, TP7, PO7, and the A1 + A2 as reference.)
Equipamento: CONTACT Precision Instruments amplifiers and DATAQ AD Converter 30 electrodes 10/20 system
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: EEG 30 electrodes , 10/20 system
Pre_Processamento: The researchers preprocessed the EEG data by filtering it into various frequency bands. They then used the Phase Lag Index (PLI) to create functional connectivity graphs. Finally, they analyzed network properties like local and global efficiency to compare a resting state with a mental arithmetic task, using both static and dynamic approaches.
Faixas_Frequencia: EEG bands: Delta(1-4Hz), Theta(4-8Hz), alfa1(8-10Hz), alfa2(10-13Hz),Beta1(13-20Hz),Beta20-30Hz) e gama(30-45Hz).
Software_Analise: MATLAB for down-sample(frequency reduction for aduls and to implement MDS
Abordagem_Estatistica: Wilcoxon rank-sum test (WRS-test) to assess differences between groups,Repeated-measures ANOVA was also employed to investigate developmental factors.False Discovery Rate (FDR) method. A difference was considered statistically significant when the p-value was extremely low, such as p < 0.001 or p < 0.0001, after correction.
Classificacao_ML: Multi-Dimensional Scaling (MDS), Markov Chain

Titulo: The neural differences of arithmetic verification performance depend on math skill: Evidence from event-related potential
Autores: Shiva Taghizadeh1 | Touraj Hashemi2 | Ali Jahan3 | Mohammad Ali Nazari1
Amostra_Info: 36 adults right-randed, 19 W 17 M ~23,5 Y
Marcadores_Amostra: habilidade matemática
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic verification task.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: The behavioral variables collected in the study to assess participant performance were Reaction Time (RT) and Accuracy (ACC). Reaction Time measured the time that each volunteer took to judge if the equation was correct or incorrect . Accuracy was the percentage of correct answers for each participant, calculated by the formula: (correct answers / total answers) x100
Posicao_Eletrodos: 10-10 system, linked mastoids as reference
Equipamento: EEG WaveGuard (ANT Company) with 64 Ag/AgCl electrodes, 64 channels amplifier, Stimulus system eevoke
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: EG 64 Ag/AgCl electrodes, 10-10 system, stimulus system eevoke
Pre_Processamento: After pre-processing, the main signal analysis was the extraction of Event-Related Potentials (ERPs). This was done by segmenting the data into epochs (from -200 ms to 1000 ms relative to the stimulus) and then averaging the trials for each condition and participant, which generated an average waveform. The final analysis consisted of measuring the peak amplitude of the negativity in the 200-400 ms time window..
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA to compare the values between the groups and conditions.
Notas: Inicialmente, 47 estudantes participaram da pesquisa, mas os dados de 11 deles foram excluídos da análise final devido a um excesso de artefatos (ruídos) nos registros de EEG.

Pre_Processamento: ERS/ERD=((A−R)/R)∗100 ( Rest phase ( R) Active phase (A) for each signal)

Notas: Por mais que não seja dito, a Figura 2, que mostra o mapa do escalpo com todos os eletrodos, exibe a disposição exata do Sistema 10-20. // fase de repouso foi definida como o intervalo de 1000 ms antes da apresentação do problema (durante a cruz de fixação).A fase ativa foi o período entre o início do problema e o momento em que o participante pressionava o botão, ou seja, o tempo de cálculo. Titulo: Analysis of long range dependence in the EEG signals of Alzheimer patients
Autores: T. Nimmy John,Subha D. Puthankattil,Ramshekhar Menon
Amostra_Info: The study was conducted with a control group of 27 healthy volunteers (15 men and 12 women) and a group of 13 patients with mild cognitive impairment (six men and seven women). All participants were between 50 and 80 years old. CSR <= 1
Marcadores_Amostra: Mild Cognitive Impairment-Alzheimer's Disease (MCI-AD) patients (CDR $\le 1$) and healthy controls.
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic eyes open and closed
Comparacao_Tarefa: Resting conditions (Eyes Open - EO and Eyes Closed - EC)
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) Rey Auditory Verbal Learning Test (RAVLT) Addenbrooke's Cognitive Examination (ACE) Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR) scale. To determine the level of cognitive impairment, the 30-point Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) was administered.The 100-point Addenbrooke's Cognitive Examination (ACE), where a lower score indicates disease progression.
Posicao_Eletrodos: International 10-20 electrode placement system.Local Anterior Region (Frontal): Calculated using electrode pairs Fp1-F3, Fp1-F7, Fp2-F4, and Fp2-F8./ Local Posterior Region: Calculated using electrode pairs P3-O1, P4-O2, T5-O1, and T6-O2. /Distant Regions: Determined by analyzing the connections between 12 different electrode pairs, such as Fp1-P3, Fp2-P4, F3-T5, and F4-T6.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: EEG 32 Channels(23 electrodelocations: Fp1, Fp2, F3, F4, C3, C4, P3, P4, O1,O2,T1, T2, F7, F8, T3, T4, T5, T6, Fz, Cz, Pz, A1 and A2.)
Pre_Processamento: Hurst Exponent (HE): This was the primary feature analyzed in the study. The HE is a nonlinear parameter used to measure the long-range dependence, self-similarity, and smoothness of a time series. It was calculated using the rescaled range (R/S) method. Auto Mutual Information: This feature was calculated to measure the complexity of the EEG signal.Cross Mutual Information(CMI): CMI was used to assess the dynamic coupling and information transmission between two different systems. This was applied to measure connectivity between different brain regions and to analyze the relationship between HE and neuropsychological scores.
Faixas_Frequencia: Gamma: 25-50 Hz Beta: 12.5-25 Hz Alpha: 6.25-12.5 Hz Theta: 3.125-6.25 Hz Delta: 0-3.125 Hz
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA ,Correlation Coefficients: To analyze the statistical relationships between the extracted features and behavioral variables, the study used:Pearson's Correlation Coefficient to measure linear relationships.
Notas: meio estranho de achar o equipamento, Nicolet tem varios modelos.(https://www.neuroswiss.ch/view/data/5962/Nicolet-Viking-EDX.pdf)

Titulo: EEG alpha and theta time-frequency structure during a written mathematical task
Autores: Giovanna M. Bonança, Günther J.L. Gerhardt, André L. Molan, Luiz M.A. Oliveira, Gustavo M. Jarola, Suzana V. Schönwald e José L. Rybarczyk-Filho
Amostra_Info: The study sample consisted of sixty healthy volunteers with a mean age of 21.9 years and a standard deviation 29 males,and the sample demonstrated good overall performance on the math task, achieving a median score of 7.
Processos_Matematicos: The mathematical process was not purely mental arithmetic; it was a written multiplication task.
Comparacao_Tarefa: Resting baseline
Variaveis_Comportamentais: The score for each math problem ranged from 0 to 10, as each correctly solved line (4 multiplications plus 1 sum) was worth 2 points.A final average score from 0 to 10 was ascribed to each subject.There was overall good performance from the sample, with a median score of 7.
Posicao_Eletrodos: Electrodes aligned with the 10-10 International System. (AF3, AF4), anterior temporal (F7, F8), frontal (F3, F4),(FC5, FC6), (T7, T8), (P7, P8), (01, 02) sites. The authors note an exception for the T7 and T8 electrodes, which are placed more posteriorly on the 10-10 system for this specific device. The mastoid electrodes M1 and M2 were used for reference; M1 functioned as the main reference point, while M2 served as a feed-forward reference to reduce external electrical interference.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: EEG 16 channels(2 for reference).
Pre_Processamento: Spectral Power analysis was conducted to provide a general overview of the EEG signal's main characteristics for PSD. Matching Pursuit (MP) based signal analysis technique, which is a algorithm that decomposes the EEG signal into a series of elements called "atoms". Each atom represents a structure in the time-frequency plane, defined by its frequency, length, and intensity using Gabor functions for decomposition. For PSD mean power in bands theta (4 Hz a 7,5 Hz) alfa (7,5 Hz a 13 Hz). For MP , Number of Atoms per seconds for each band. Atom Lenght: Duration of each atom had a mean of 3 seconds. Atom Frequency and peak amplitude.
Faixas_Frequencia: (Theta): Definida no intervalo de 4 Hz a 7,5 Hz. (Alpha): Definida no intervalo de 7,5 Hz a 13 Hz.
Abordagem_Estatistica: To make comparisons between the left and right hemispheres and between the Rest and Math conditions, two-tailed Wilcoxon rank-sum tests were implemented. To correct for multiple comparisons, the study applied a False Discovery Rate (FDR) correction, and FDR-corrected p-values (q-values) of less than 0.05 were considered significant.
Notas: *The authors acknowledge that the fact it was a written task, involving hand movement, could have confounded the EEG results.*

Titulo: Localization of neural efficiency of the mathematically gifted brain through a feature subset selection method
Autores: Li Zhang1 • John Q. Gan1,2 • Haixian Wang1
Amostra_Info: 2 groups, all right handed subjects: math-gifted group, 8 adolescents (five males and three females) aged 16.5 ± 0.7; control group, 7 students (five males and two females) aged 16.3 ± 0.8; Raven Advanced Progressive Matrices (RAPM) scores respectively above and below 32
Marcadores_Amostra: Mathematical Giftedness / Level of Mathematical Ability
Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic, addition and subtraction of single digits with key-pressing responses
Comparacao_Tarefa: Pre-stimulus interval baseline
Variaveis_Comportamentais: RAPM score, response accuracy, response time, Mean value of global GBRs
Posicao_Eletrodos: all 60-ch 10-10 IS brain regions
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 60-channel 10-10 IS; 4 EOGs

SFFS algorithm (Pudil et al. 1994)

Titulo: The Role of Mathematical and Trait Anxiety in Mental Fatigue: an EEG Investigation
Autores: Ekaterina S. Zhban*a , Maxim V. Likhanovb , Ilya M. Zakharovc Elizaveta, M. Bezrukovaa, Sergey B. Malykhc
Revista: Psychology in Russia: State of the Art
Amostra_Info: 101 participants with mean age of 21.62 (SD=4.28; 33 males, 68 females) (nested on an ongoing twin study)
Marcadores_Amostra: Mathematics Anxiety (MA) and Trait Anxiety (TA) levels
Processos_Matematicos: arithmetic and algebra
Comparacao_Tarefa: Resting state
Variaveis_Psicologicas: STAI (Spielberger Trait Anxiety Questionnaire) and sMARS (Shortened Math Anxiety Rating Scale)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Math anxiety by Shortened Math Anxiety Rating Scale (sMARS), 21-item Russian adaptation; Trait anxiety by a Russian adaptation of the Spielberger Trait Anxiety Questionnaire; The studied factor was a mixed-type cognitive task consisting of math problems and subjective ratings about anxiety. The outcome was the EEG change
Posicao_Eletrodos: Frontais e linha média anterior
Equipamento: Hardware not mentioned. EEG acquisition software: BrainVision PyCorder software (Brain Products); 64 electrodes; extended 10–20 system;
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: Fz, F3, F4, F7, F8, Cz and Pz.
Pre_Processamento: PSD (Power Spectral Density) FFT averaged for 150 2s windows
Faixas_Frequencia: alpha (8-12 Hz), beta (12-30 Hz), and theta (4-7 Hz); ratios (quocients): 1. relative alpha spectral power = alpha spectral power /(alpha spectral power plus theta spectral power + beta spectral power); 2. relative theta spectral power = theta spectral power /(alpha spectral power plus theta spectral power + beta spectral power); 3. relative beta spectral power = beta spectral power /(alpha spectral power plus theta spectral power + beta spectral power). Additionally, two composite measures for fatigue: Task Load Index (TLI), ratio of theta activity at frontal midline sites, to alpha at parietal sites (here theta at Fz to alpha at Pz); expected to increase with the development of mental fatigue. Second, Relative Energy Index (REI), calculated as ratio of (alpha + theta) to beta activity.
Abordagem_Estatistica: Descriptive statistics and Wilcoxon Signed Rank Tests for absolute and relative activity and quocients; rho correlations for EEGfrequency bands and electrodes vs subjective anxiety ratings
Classificacao_ML: None (no machine learning used).

Titulo: Desynchronization of Theta-Phase Gamma-Amplitude Coupling during a Mental Arithmetic Task in Children with Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder
Autores: Jun Won Kim1, Bung-Nyun Kim2, Jaewon Lee3, Chul Na4, Baik Seok Kee4, Kyung Joon Min4, Doug Hyun Han4 , Johanna Inhyang Kim2 , Young Sik Lee4
Amostra_Info: 97 volunteers (including 53 subjects with ADHD) from a camp for hyperactive children, DISC-IV criteria
Marcadores_Amostra: Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) in children
Processos_Matematicos: arithmetic (serial subtractions)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Eyes-closed resting state
Variaveis_Psicologicas: IVA CPT (Intermediate Visual and Auditory Continuous Performance Test), DISC-IV (Korean version of the Diagnostic Interview Schedule for Children Version IV), k-WISC-IV (Korean-Wechsler Intelligence Scale for Children, Fourth Edition)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Behavioral TDAH severity: Intermediate Visual and Auditory (IVA) continuous performance test (CPT) scores; Studied factor: rest vs math task; Outcome: EEG variables (PSD, TGC)
Posicao_Eletrodos: 21 IS scalp electrodes not specifically mentioned and analysed as a whole
Equipamento: EEG: SynAmps2 direct-current (DC) amplifier; headcap: 10–20 layout 64-channel Quick-Cap electrode placement system (Neuroscan Inc., NC, USA); linked mastoid reference and two additional bipolar EOG electrodes
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 electrodes (10–20 IS)
Pre_Processamento: PSD: (Power Spectral Density) FFT (“spectrogram.m” function) on Time windows of 1000-ms with an 800-ms overlap and the Hamming window. more than two minutes of artifact-free EEG readings from the three-minute recordings. TGC: a phase coherence measurement; synchronization index (SI) proposed by Cohen [29] to assess the cross-frequency interactions between the low-frequency (4–8 Hz) phase and the power of the gamma (39–41 Hz) oscillations. gamma power time series was extracted as the squared magnitude of z(t), which is the analytic signal obtained from the Hilbert transform (power time series: p(t) = real[z(t)]2 + imag[z(t)]2 ). The phases of the two time series were extracted from the Hilbert transform (phase 1⁄4 arctan imag1⁄2zðtÞ real1⁄2zðtÞ ).
Faixas_Frequencia: delta (1–4 Hz), theta (4–8 Hz), slow alpha (8–10 Hz), fast alpha (10–13.5 Hz), and beta (13.5–30 Hz).
Abordagem_Estatistica: The paired t-test, Bonferroni correction, (ANCOVA) to compare the two conditions in each frequency band and TGC between groups; Pearson’s partial correlation analysis that controlled for sex and age to assess the relationship between the measures of IVA CPT and the EEG recordings
Classificacao_ML: None (no machine learning used).

Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic (serial subtractions)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Resting state
Variaveis_Comportamentais: 1) Individual task performance: number of arithmetic subtraction operations per minute. 2) Group performance: Good and BAD groups, BAD of 10 participants, (mean no. of operations = 6.2 ± 3.37), the task was found difficult; GOOD 26 participants, (mean no. of operations = 21.99 ± 7.46), task managed without difficulty. 3) Quality of task performance: estimated based on the comparative correct results for each participant; if the participants’ reported result was around 20% of the correct value, then it is considered a successful engagement of that participant.
Posicao_Eletrodos: All: anterior frontal, frontal, central, parietal, occipital and temporal
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 electrodes (10–20 IS) i) anterior frontal (Fp1, Fp2), ii) frontal (F3, F4, Fz, F7, F8), iii) central (C3, C4, Cz), iv) parietal (P3, P4, Pz), v) occipital (O1, O2), and vi) temporal (T3, T4, T5, T6)
Pre_Processamento: EMD (empirical mode decomposition) extracted signal features. EMD generates a highly localized time-frequency estimation of a signal in the modulated oscillatory pattern by decomposing it into a finite sum of intrinsic mode functions (IMF) combined with the Hilbert–Huang transforms (HHT). It is data-adaptive and does not require any hand-crafted functions as well as it extracts the maximum characteristics of EEG over different frequency sub-bands. EEG sample: first 3 minutes of experimental or resting adaption) and the first minute (out of 4 minutes) of the mental arithmetic task.
Faixas_Frequencia: not reported
Software_Analise: MATLAB, fit classification error-correcting output code (‘fitcecoc’)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Descriptive analysis of the machine-learning group classification performance
Classificacao_ML: Support vector machine (SVM) model in a subject-independent approach. For classification purposes, several classifiers are initially deployed such as k-Nearest Neighbor, Naive Bayes, Decision Tree, SVM, and Linear Discriminant Analysis. However, SVM is used for the rest analysis of this work, as initially, SVM showed the highest performance. Trained the model with the first 20 subjects’ data, and the rest 16 data were reserved for testing.
Acesso_Dados: 3, already pre-processed, ‘PhysioNet’ available through the ‘PhysioBank’ platform

Titulo: The Impact of Math Anxiety on Working Memory: A Cortical Activations and Cortical Functional Connectivity EEG Study
Autores: MANOUSOS A. KLADOS 1,2, (Member, IEEE), EVANGELOS PARASKEVOPOULOS2, NIKI PANDRIA2, , AND PANAGIOTIS D. BAMIDIS 2 , (Member, IEEE)
Amostra_Info: (2) groups of 16 students of the Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, namely LMA (Low Math Anxiety), and HMA (High Math Anxiety) according to their AMAS scores; mean ages were 22.5 ± 2.3 (LMA) and 22.21 ± 2.43 (HMA)
Marcadores_Amostra: High-Math Anxious (HMA) and Low-Math Anxious (LMA) groups based on AMAS scores
Processos_Matematicos: visual digit comparison
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Performance: 1) correctness of the given answers by means of the d’prime index; 2) reaction time, extracted by the stimuli presentation software. Other anxiety measures: STAI I (State Anxiety) and II (Trait Anxiety) tests. Math task: n-back task in 3 levels: 1) in the 1-Back Test, to press the right mouse-button, if the presented number was the same as the one shown previously, or the left one otherwise; 2) (and 3): to press the button presented number was the same with the number appeared two (three) positions before. EEG outcome variables:
Posicao_Eletrodos: All: anterior frontal, frontal, central, parietal, occipital and temporal
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 57 scalp electrodes 10-10 IS
Pre_Processamento: Magnitude square coherence, current density reconstructions. Cortical activity: 0,5-45 Hz sLORETA (Low Resolution Electromagnetic Tomography) Current density reconstructions (CDRs) (performed with a Boundary Element Model method (BEM) using a Brainstorm generic, 4-compartment head model) on 3 fixed time windows (centered at 200, 300 and 400ms). Functional connectivity: Magnitude Square Coherence (MSC): square of the cross Power Spectrum Density (PSD) calculated within each pair of vertices. PSD estimated using the Welch method for Discrete Fourier Transform.
Faixas_Frequencia: 6 frequency bands: delta (0.5-4Hz), theta (4-8Hz), alpha1 (8-10Hz), alpha2 (10-12Hz), beta (12-30Hz), gamma (30-45Hz)]
Software_Analise: EEGLAB, Brainstorm toolbox, MATLAB Brain Connectivity Toolbox (MSC function, etc.); stats: Statistical Parametric Mapping 8 (SPM8) and GLM-Flex for CDRs reconstructions; MRIcron for visualization of statistical parametric maps
Abordagem_Estatistica: A 2 × 3 mixed model ANOVA with the group category (LMA and HMA) as the between subject factor and the BT tasks (BT1; BT2 and BT3) as the within subject factor. FDR correction emplyoed.
Classificacao_ML: None (no machine learning used).

Autores: Zheng Yang Chin, Xin Zhang, Chuanchu Wang, Kai Keng Ang
Amostra_Info: 10 healthy subjects performing mental addition with 3 difficulty levels (easy, medium and hard)
Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic (mental addition)

Titulo: Stereotype-based stressors facilitate emotional memory neural network connectivity and encoding of negative information to degrade math self-perceptions among women
Autores: Chad E. Forbes, Rachel Amey, Adam B. Magerman, Kelly Duran, and Mengting Liu
Amostra_Info: 160 white paid participants (87 women) aware of the negative female math stereotype, sample randomized into 2 groups with different interventions: 1) stereotype threat/DMT condition or 2) control/problem-solving (PST) condition
Marcadores_Amostra: Gender (Women vs. Men) and Context (Stereotype Threat/DMT vs. Control/PST)
Processos_Matematicos: arithmetic and probably other types of problems
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Self-Attributes Questionnaire (SAQ) for math self-enhancement and Math Devaluing scale
Variaveis_Comportamentais: 1) math feedback task: standard multiplication and division problems with simple choice answers; 2) standard math task: 15 difficult math word problems taken from the GRE, paper written answers; 3) surprise memory test containing font/feedback pairs they had previously seen during the math feedback task; 4) math self-assessments on a modified shortened version of Self-Attributes Questionnaire (SAQ); 5) Math devaluing 5-item questionnaire; 6) EEG variables; 7) white noise startle responses during the math feedback test; In sum, 8 studied variables: 1) Math feedback and2) standard Math scores, 3) memory accuracy for negative and 4) positive feedback; 5) Average peak startle of negative and 6) positive feedback; 7) Math devaluing; 8) Self-enhancement. Outcomes seem to be statistical (ANOVA) performance on these tests and functional network connectivity (descriptively analyzed).
Posicao_Eletrodos: All, high resolution: anterior frontal, frontal, central, parietal, occipital and temporal (68 anatomical regions of interest (ROIs)
Equipamento: ActiveTwo head, cap and the ActiveTwo Biosemi system
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 128 electrodes
Pre_Processamento: EEG source reconstruction ; functional connectivity estimation by phase-locking values; weighted graphs construction; measurement of select network modularity;
Faixas_Frequencia: teta: 4–8 Hz, alpha: 9–14 Hz, beta: 18–22 Hz, gamma: 25–50 Hz
Software_Analise: EEG: ActiView software (BioSemi); source reconstruction: MNE-python; calculated via forward and inverse models utilized by MNE-python; weighted graphs construction: Brain Connectivity Toolbox;
Abordagem_Estatistica: An initial 2 (Gender: Men or Women) 2 (Condition: DMT or PST) factorial ANOVA was conducted on participants’ accuracy on the math feedback task. Contrasts were also conducted to compare DMT women’s performance on the math feedback task to the other three conditions. For each matrix the threshold was set statistically, so that only the largest 25% of values for edges out of all possible edge values were left for each graph. From this, two sub networks were isolated in the labels of interest from the full sparse graph representing the emotional memory and semantic memory networks. In sum, 8 studied variables: 1) Math feedback and2) standard Math scores, 3) memory accuracy for negative and 4) positive feedback; 5) Average peak startle of negative and 6) positive feedback; 7) Math devaluing; 8) Self-enhancement. Outcomes seem to be statistical (ANOVA) performance on these tests and functional network connectivity (descriptively analyzed).
Classificacao_ML: None (no machine learning used).
Notas: it should be noted that, although the spatial reconstruction localizes activity within the region of the amigdala and other deep sources, it is not proven that deeper brain activity can be recorded on scalp EEG; it most likely requires depth electrodes to be recorded. For instance, this paper cites a model, theoretical study! (Attal and Schwartz, 2013). Pending issue: to find electrophysiological validation for this type of inference.

Titulo: Math anxiety and the shifting function: An event-related potential study of arithmetic task switching
Autores: Belén Gonzalez-G omez 1,2 | M. Isabel Núñez-Peña1,2,3 | Àngels Colomé2,4
Amostra_Info: 40 students, 39 right-handed, (22 female and 18 male) selected from a 827 sample assessed for trait anxiety and math anxiety, organized in 2 groups of 20: low and high math anxiety
Marcadores_Amostra: Highly Math-Anxious (HMA) and Low Math-Anxious (LMA) groups
Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic (two-digit additions and subtractions) with right/left mouse button pressing with left or right thumb!!
Variaveis_Comportamentais: 1) Math anxiety: sMARS; 2) State–Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI); 3) working memory span (computerized Corsi test). Studied variable: Math test performance in low vs high anxiety, switch assymetry, etc.; outcome: EEG ERPs
Posicao_Eletrodos: All: anterior frontal, frontal, central, parietal, occipital and temporal, plus midline
Equipamento: Scan 4.5 hardware and software (Compumedics Neuroscan, ware and software (Compumedics Neuroscan, (VA)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 32 electrodes: 8 midline sagittal plane sites at Fpz, Fz, FCz, Cz, CPz, Pz, POz and Oz; 12 lateral pairs of electrodes over standard sites in the prefrontal (FP1/FP2), frontal (F3/F4, F7/F8), frontocentral (FC3/FC4), frontotemporal (FT7/FT8), central (C3/C4), temporal (T7/T8), centroparietal (CP3/CP4), temporoparietal (TP7/TP8), parietal (P3/P4, P7/P8) and occipital (O1/O2) positions, and with the ground electrode located between FPz and Fz. A further two electrodes were then placed at the mastoids and used later for re-referencing. Finally, the common reference electrode was placed on the tip of the nose.
Pre_Processamento: ERPs (Event-Related Potentials): cue-locked epochs (100 ms to 900 ms) for each experimental condition of every participant, relative to a pre-stimulus baseline of 100 ms
Faixas_Frequencia: .5 to 70 Hz (ERPs do not analyze separate frequency bands, they analyze power in tiny time windows)
Software_Analise: Math problem generation: MATLAB; task design and presentation: E-Prime 2.0 software (Psychology Software Tools Inc., Sharpsburg, PA); stats: SPSSv24; EEG preprocessing: EEGLAB; ERP extraction: ERPLAB;
Abordagem_Estatistica: an initial t test for trait-anxiety between groups; descriptive stats; only accurate trials were included. These were first examined using ANOVA, taking Cue Type (Repeat vs. Switch) and Operation Addition vs. subtraction) as the within-subject factors, and Group (low math-anxious vs. highly math-anxious) as the between-subjects factor. Third, to compute the switch effect per group and operation, the difference value obtained when subtracting the median response time in repeating trials from the median response time in switching trials was calculated for each participant and for both operations. Switch effects were submitted to t-tests in order to evaluate whether they differ from zero. Fourth, Spearman’s correlation was used to examine the association between the participants’ scores on the sMARS and their mean switch effect. Finally, to examine differences in accuracy between groups and to determine whether there was any switch cost in accuracy, hit rate (i.e., the proportion of trials responded accurately for each condition in each participant) was also analysed. The same statistical analyses run in response time were performed for hit rate: an ANOVA was carried out, taking Cue Type and Operation as the within-subject factors, and Group as the between-subjects factor, and the switch effect (difference value between Cue Type conditions) was submitted to a t-test for each operation and group; Hochberg correction for multiple comparisons.
Classificacao_ML: None (no machine learning used).

Titulo: Math on cortex—enhanced delta phase synchrony in math experts during long and complex math demonstrations
Autores: Hanna Poikonen *, Samuel Tobler1, Dragan Trninic ́ 1, Cléa Formaz1, Venera Gashaj1,3, Manu Kapur1
Amostra_Info: Two right-handed groups: 22 math experts, (5 female and 17 male, 19 to 24 years), and 22 math novices (7 female and 15 male, 19 to 35 years). ANOVA age effect that would have to be controlled along the study
Marcadores_Amostra: Math Experts
Processos_Matematicos: watching complex Math demonstrations with symbolic and geometric approaches, the response was self-evaluation with 4-choice button pressing
Comparacao_Tarefa: Resting baseline
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Abbreviated Math Anxiety Scale (AMAS), Berlin Intelligence Structure Test (BIS-T)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: 1) Math demonstrations in 4 experimental conditions as described in column K; 2) Abbreviated Math Anxiety Scale (AMAS); 3) shorter version of the Berlin Intelligence Structure Test (BIS-T); 4)
Posicao_Eletrodos: both hemispheres and the central line along the fronto-parietal pathway
Equipamento: Ant Neuro eego mylab with 128 channel amplifier (IS 10-10)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 12 electrodes of F3 (5), Fz (6), F4 (7), FCz (42), Cz (16), CP3 (48), CP4 (49), P1 (51), Pz (26), P2 (52), PPO1 (92) and PPO2; pairwise to all the other ones, resulting in 66 electrode pairs of comparison.
Pre_Processamento: phase synchrony values (PSVs) based on the Hilbert transformation (EEG functional connectiviity examined by phase synchrony), studying separately 1) the beginning of stimuli to avoid habituation and study a similar duration as in previous EEG studies (2s or less), and 2) the whole stimuli to study the brain processes during complex math with long duration (54s);
Faixas_Frequencia: delta 0.5–4 Hz, theta and alpha.
Software_Analise: Stimulus presentations: MATLAB via PsychToolbox; triggers sent wirelessly via Lab Streaming Layer; EEG preprocessing by EEGLAB; stats: MATLAB
Abordagem_Estatistica: The repeated-measures ANOVA with between-subject factor Group (Math Experts and Novices) and within-subject factors Posture (Sitting Eyes Open and Standing Eyes Open for the resting state and Sitting and Standing for math demonstrations, and Stimulus: Symbolic and geometric math demonstration), was conducted separately for each electrode pair (66 electrode pairs) of PSV_FirstTwoSeconds and PSV_WholeStimulus, each frequency band (delta, theta and alpha) and both during math demonstrations and rest. The main effects for the factor Group, Posture and Stimulus, and the Group∗Posture, Group∗Stimulus interactions were calculated with the Greenhouse–Geisser (GG) adjustment. Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons.
Classificacao_ML: None (no machine learning used).

Titulo: EEG source-space synchrostate transitions and Markov modeling in the math-gifted brain during a long-chain reasoning task
Autores: Li Zhang1 | John Q. Gan2 | Yanmei Zhu3 | Jing Wang4 | Haixian Wang3
Amostra_Info: Math-gifted group: 12 subjects including nine males and three females aged 16.5 ± 0.7 (mean ± SD), selected from the Science and Engineering Experimental Class at Southeast University, Nanjing, China.
Marcadores_Amostra: Mathematical Giftedness / Level of Mathematical Ability
Processos_Matematicos: a standard type of deductive reasoning
Comparacao_Tarefa: Non-task idle process
Variaveis_Comportamentais: 1) a standard type of deductive reasoning, a categorical syllogism task of verbal-logical type was performed by all the subjects;
Posicao_Eletrodos: frontal, parietal, temporal, and occipital regions
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 60 electrodes covering frontal, parietal, temporal, and occipital regions.
Pre_Processamento: Phase-locking value (PLV) estimation. Sources mapped to a distributed model composed of 151 dipoles uniformly covering the surface of dorsal and ventral cortices, which were used as the fundamental nodes for functional brain network analysis. 180 discrete epochs, in which phase-synchronized networks of cortical sources sustained for every 50 ms time windows (i.e., synchrostates) were constructed along the reasoning task course and the task-free baseline process. state-dependent functional connectivity: single-trial source data were used in the analysis of phase-locking statistics. For each PLV association matrix of size 151 × 151, an adjacent matrix can be acquired by applying a fixed connection density (FCV).
Faixas_Frequencia: low γ frequency band (30–45 Hz)
Software_Analise: EEG pre-processing: Scan 4.3 software, EEGLAB; source transformation procedure: Brainstorm toolbox; forward model: obtained according to a symmetric Boundary Element Model in OpenMEEG software; inverse solution: minimum-norm estimation (MNE) and sLORETA;
Abordagem_Estatistica: As the most fundamental measure of functional connectivity within a brain connectome, node degree centrality quantifies the structural importance of a node with respect to the rest of the network, especially stronger effects within its own cluster. Cosine similarity tests for reallocating synchrostates into optimal clusters. Analysis of variance (AVOVA) between task conditions and between subject groups. bootstrapped ANOVAs for transition probabilities testing. (FDR) correction.
Classificacao_ML: Markov chain modeling for synchrostate transitions

Titulo: Interpretability of Hybrid Feature Using Graph Neural Networks from Mental Arithmetic Based EEG
Autores: Jung, M. K., Kim, H., Lee, S., Kim, J. B., & Kim, D. J.
Amostra_Info: 29 healthy subjects (14 males, 15 females) with an average age of 28.5 +- 3.7 years from an open source database
Processos_Matematicos: repeatedly perform simple arithmetic calculations.
Comparacao_Tarefa: resting baseline
Variaveis_Comportamentais: 1) rest state; 2) mental calculations;
Posicao_Eletrodos: all regions with an electrode placement I am not familiar with
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 30 electrodes placed according to the international 10-5 system (nunca ouvi falar). Mencionam: frontal channels (AFp1, AFp2, AFF1h, AFF2h, AFF5h, AFF6h, F3, F4, F7, and F8); parieto-occipital lesion (P4, P8, PPO2h, POO2, Pz, POO1, PPO1h, P3, and P7); frontal regions (AFF1h, AFp1, AFF5h, and F3),
Pre_Processamento: Power spectral density (PSD) . for extracting node features, the node strength and power spectral density were used.
Faixas_Frequencia: delta (1-4 Hz), theta (4-8 Hz), alpha (8-12 Hz), beta (12-30 Hz), and gamma (30-40Hz) frequency bands utilized to derive node features
Abordagem_Estatistica: connectivity derived by the envelope correlation method ; To compute envelope correlation, analytic signals were obtained by applying the Hilbert transform, and the mean of Pearson correlation between orthogonalizing values of two signals was calculated. To compare the effect of PSD features on model performance, a dataset with connectivity (CON) only and a dataset with connectivity and PSD features (CON-PSD) were generated. GNNExplainer is to generate a subgraph that explains the decision.
Classificacao_ML: GNN model. inter-node relations were evaluated via adjacency matrices that represent connectivity, distance, and degree information. In addition, the node strength was used as node feature. GNN model trained by optimizing the negative log-likelihood loss function using Adam and backpropagation. To identify and classify the MA task-related brain activity patterns, this study utilized hierarchical graph pooling with a structure learning model (HGP-SL).
Acesso_Dados: 3 J. Shin et al., "Open access dataset for EEG+ NIRS single-trial classification," IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering,

Titulo: (Lack of) neural efficiency related to general giftedness and mathematical excellence: An EEG study
Autores: Waisman, Ilana and Brunner, Clemens and Grabner, Roland H. and Leikin, Mark and Leikin, Roza
Amostra_Info: 118 high school students (45 females, 73 males) with a mean age of 16.6 years (standard deviation 0.6).
Marcadores_Amostra: General Giftedness (G) and Excellence in Mathematics (EM)
Processos_Matematicos: learning-based mathematical task that requires the competence to translate between graphical and symbolic representations of functions.
Comparacao_Tarefa: 500 ms fixation period
Variaveis_Comportamentais: accuracy, reaction time for correct reponses
Posicao_Eletrodos: extended 10–20 system. full
Equipamento: BioSemi ActiveTwo system (BioSemi, Amsterdam, The Netherlands). 64-channel
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 electrodes
Pre_Processamento: Bandpass-filtered between 0.53 and 30 Hz. Referenced to the common average of all electrodes. Epochs with signals exceeding ±80 µV were discarded. Ocular artifacts were corrected using a regression-based approach. Downsampled to 256 Hz. Features extracted: Event-related (de-)synchronization (ERD/ERS), calculated as the percentage change in power from a baseline period.
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (4–7 Hz). Upper Alpha (10–12 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA, Greenhouse-Geisser, Bonferroni adjustment was used for post-hoc tests.

Titulo: Alpha and theta oscillations in mental addition for high and low performers
Autores: Alnajashi, Sumyah
Amostra_Info: 36 female undergraduate students. High Performers: 18 participants, mean age 21 years and 3 months. Low Performers: 18 participants, mean age 20 years and 8 months.
Marcadores_Amostra: female, arithmetic fluency scores
Processos_Matematicos: mental addition task involving problems with either single-digit or two-digit integers.
Comparacao_Tarefa: baseline period (500ms pre-stimulus). The study also compares the task under various conditions: easy vs. difficult problems, horizontal vs. vertical presentation, and with vs. without a visuospatial load.
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Digit Span (serial and reversed recall) score (WAIS/WISC). Coding task score (WAIS/WISC), RPMT
Variaveis_Comportamentais: accuracy, self-reported strategies (procedural or recall).
Posicao_Eletrodos: anterior frontal, frontal, central, parietal
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 14 recording electrodes plus 2 references. Disposable felt pads soaked with saline.
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtered from 0.5 to 45 Hz. 50 Hz and 60 Hz notch filters were applied. Epochs from 500 ms pre-stimulus to 2500 ms post-stimulus were extracted. Titulo: Electrophysiological markers of poor versus superior math abilities in healthy individuals
Autores: Proverbio, Alice M. and Carminati, Manuel
Amostra_Info: 26 individuals were selected for the EEG recordings and were subdivided into two groups of 13, representing the best ("skilled") and worst ("poor") performers. The final sample included in the analysis consisted of 22 participants.
Marcadores_Amostra: The primary marker was arithmetic ability, which was used to divide the sample into two groups.
Processos_Matematicos: The study investigated the cognitive processes involved in solving basic arithmetical operations, specifically additions, subtractions, multiplications, and divisions. The core mathematical task was a verification paradigm in which participants were presented with an arithmetic problem followed by a proposed solution and had to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether that solution was correct or incorrect.
Comparacao_Tarefa: The brain activity (ERP) evoked by the presentation of a correct solution is directly compared to the brain activity evoked by the presentation of an incorrect solution.Therefore, the comparison is not with a task of a different nature (such as reading or a resting state), but rather a comparison between two conditions within the arithmetic paradigm itself.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy,Reaction Time,Response Hand
Posicao_Eletrodos: Fz,FCz,Cz 10-5, frontal, central
Equipamento: Based on the methodology described in the article, the EEG recording was performed using the EEvoke software package, manufactured by ANT (Enschede), with a 128-channel system. The article does not specify the model of the amplifier or the electrode cap.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: The EEG was recorded using 128 channels (or "scalp sites") with the average mastoids serving as the physical reference lead. The electrode cap layout was arranged according to the international 10-5 system.
Pre_Processamento: Based on the article's methodology, the EEG data preprocessing involved a 0.016-100 Hz band-pass filter,the continuous data was segmented into epochs from -100 to 1,500 ms. The feature extraction focused exclusively on Event-Related Potentials (ERPs), specifically measuring the mean area amplitude and the peak latency of the P300 component as the primary features.
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA applied separately to both the EEG data (P300 amplitude and latency) and the behavioral data (reaction times and hit percentages).

Titulo: Enhanced learning of proportional math through music training and spatial-temporal training
Autores: Graziano AB and Peterson M and Shaw GL
Amostra_Info: The article details the samples for two separate studies: a pilot and a main study. The pilot study included 102 second-grade children with an age range of six years eight months to eight years five months. For the four-month main study, researchers recruited 170 second-graders , but 34 students withdrew, leaving a final sample of 136 children. The age of the participants in the main study ranged from six years eight months to eight years ten months at the time of pretesting.
Marcadores_Amostra: The pilot study drew participants from two schools with contrasting backgrounds: one was a "below-average demographics school" (School SA) , and the other was an "above-average demographics school" (IR) , with this difference being confirmed by baseline Stanford 9 math scores. In contrast, the main study's sample was drawn entirely from a single Los Angeles school (LA) described as being "below-average" in both demographics and academic achievement and was ranked among the city's 100 poorest-performing schools.
Processos_Matematicos: The central mathematical processes investigated in the article are proportional math and fractions. .
Comparacao_Tarefa: In this research, the mathematical task was primarily compared against two control conditions. The main active control was a non-numeric, verbal task where children engaged in English language training on computers, using software designed to teach reading, spelling, and writing. This was benchmarked against a baseline "No Lesson" control group that received no specific training, allowing researchers to measure the math task's effects against both a different cognitive activity and the absence of intervention.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: During the Spatial-Temporal (ST) Math Video Game training, key measures included the final score, hit/error rates, and response speed, as points were awarded for correct responses while incorrect or slow responses resulted in a point reduction.

Abordagem_Estatistica: z-value,Configuration Frequency Analysis,Bonferroni-Holm correction

Variaveis_Comportamentais: accuracy
Posicao_Eletrodos: Fp1-Fp2-F3-F4-C3-C4-P3-P4-01-02-F7-F8-T3-T4-T5-T6-Fz-Pz-Cz, international 10-20, temporal , parietal

Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 electrodes

Software_Analise: WinEEG,Neuroguide (versão 3.0.8.1),Neuronavigator,MATLAB,SPSS (versão 18.0 para Windows) WinEEG,Neuroguide (versão 3.0.8.1),Neuronavigator,MATLAB,SPSS (versão 18.0 para Windows)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Began with the Shapiro-Wilk test to evaluate the normality of the data distribution, which was rejected as the results were significant (p<0.05). Additionally, an independent sample t-test was used.
