Titulo: Gender-Dependent Frequency–Spatial Organization of the Brain Cortex Activity during Convergent and Divergent Thinking: II. Analysis of the EEG Coherence
Autores: Razoumnikova, O.M.
Amostra_Info: 66 students (36 men and 30 women); 17- to 23-year-olds
Marcadores_Amostra: Gender
Processos_Matematicos: Convergent Thinking (CTh), simulated by arithmetical addition (mental arithmetic)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Baseline conditions
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Performance efficiency (successful/unsuccessful in solving the divergent task); Subjects' responses (conveyed via microphone)
Posicao_Eletrodos: 16 symmetrical sites of the right and left hemispheres; Sites: Fp1, Fp2, F3, F4, F7, F8, C3, C4, P3, P4, T3, T4, T5, T6, O1, O2; Regions: Frontal, centro-parieto-occipital, temporal; International 10-20 System
Equipamento: Medicor encephalograph (Hungary); IBM AT-386 personal computer
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 16 electrodes; Monopolar recording; Linked earlobe electrode reference
Pre_Processamento: Time constant 0.3 s; 30-Hz low-pass filter; Analog-to-digital conversion; Analysis of artifact-free 2-s EEG segments (artifact removal implied)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Student's t-test; Analysis of variance (ANOVA) (Factors: GEN, TEST, FB, LAT, GR)

Titulo: Identifying Gifted Thinking Activities Through EEG Microstate Topology Analysis
Autores: Zhang, Li and Cao, Mingna and Shi, Bo
Revista: Lecture Notes in Computer Science (including subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics)
Amostra_Info: 15 total (8 gifted, 7 non-gifted); Gifted: 16.5 ± 0.7 years; Non-gifted: 16.3 ± 0.8 years
Marcadores_Amostra: Math-gifted adolescents
Processos_Matematicos: Deductive Reasoning (with implications for math-gifted thinking/cognitive processing)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Resting state (eyes-open) baseline (pre-stimulus periods)
Variaveis_Fisiologicas: EOG (via 4 surface electrodes monitoring ocular movements and eye blinks)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy/Error rate (judgement of conclusion correctness)
Posicao_Eletrodos: Frontal, parietal, temporal, occipital regions (covered by 60 data electrodes); International 10-20 system (implied)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 60 data electrodes; 2 reference electrodes (bilateral mastoids)
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtering (1-30 Hz); Baseline-correction; Artifacts rejection; ICA (for ocular and muscle movement artifacts); Epoching (3000 ms)
Software_Analise: Cartool EEG analysis software; EEGLAB Toolbox; Matlab Statistics Toolbox
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA (one-way); Bonferroni Corrections; Statistical testing for topological distribution
Classificacao_ML: k-means clustering; LDA; SVM; Naive Bayes classifiers; 10-fold cross validation; Accuracy

Titulo: MATHEMATICALLY GIFTED ADOLESCENTS MOBILIZE ENHANCED WORKSPACE CONFIGURATION OF THETA CORTICAL NETWORK DURING DEDUCTIVE REASONING
Autores: Zhang, L. and Gan, J.Q. and Wang, H.
Amostra_Info: 24 adolescents (11 Math-gifted, 13 Control); Ages 15-18 years (Gifted: mean $16.3 \pm 0.6$ years; Control: mean $15.9 \pm 0.7$ years)
Marcadores_Amostra: Mathematically gifted adolescents vs. Average-ability control subjects
Processos_Matematicos: Deductive Reasoning (verbal-logical categorical syllogism task)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Pre-stimulus interval/Resting state
Variaveis_Psicologicas: RAPM (Raven's Advanced Progressive Matrices) score (as an intelligence level criterion)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Reaction time of correct responses; Response accuracy
Posicao_Eletrodos: Regions: Fronto-parietal, anterior cortical vertices, frontal midline (fm theta). Positions: FZ, FCZ, F1-F8, AF3-PO3. System: Neuroscan international 10-20 system.
Equipamento: Neuroscan international 10-20 system (EEG); E-Prime 2.0 (Stimuli); Scan 4.3 software (Preprocessing)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 60 data channels; Reference: Bilateral mastoids; Layout: International 10-20 system.
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtering (1-60 Hz); Epoching (9500 ms); Baseline-correction; Ocular artifacts removal; Artifact rejection; ICA (Independent Component Analysis).
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta-band (4-8 Hz); Frequencies from 4 to 50 Hz (analyzed in time-frequency plots).
Software_Analise: Scan 4.3 software; EEGLAB toolbox; Brainstorm toolbox; OpenMEEG.
Abordagem_Estatistica: One-way ANOVA; Bootstrapping statistical test; Pearson's correlation coefficient; Louvain method (for modularity).
Classificacao_ML: LDA (Linear Discriminant Analysis); Validation: 10-fold cross validation; Metrics: Accuracy.

Titulo: Measures and models for predicting cognitive fatigue
Autores: Trejo, Leonard J. and Kochavi, Rebekah and Kubitz, Karla and Montgomery, Leslie D. and Rosipal, Roman and Matthews, Bryan 2005 Proceedings of SPIE - The International Society for Optical Engineering 10.1117/12.604286
Revista: Biomonitoring for Physiological and Cognitive Performance during Military Operations,
Amostra_Info: 16 participants (12 males, 4 females); Mean age 26.9 (SD=7.4) years (Range 18-38 y in abstract)
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic (addition and subtraction summation problems)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Time on task (e.g., first 15 minutes vs. last 15 minutes)
Variaveis_Fisiologicas: EOG (Vertical and Horizontal Electrooculograms - VEOG and HEOG)
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Activation Deactivation Adjective Checklist (AD-ACL); Visual Analogue Mood Scale (VAMS)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Response Time (RT); Response Accuracy; Observed Activity and Alertness (from videotaped recordings)
Posicao_Eletrodos: Fz (Frontal theta); Pz (Parietal alpha); O2, Fz, CPz (ERP components)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: Ag/AgCl electrodes; 32 EEG channels (embedded in a Quik-Cap™); Electronically linked mastoids reference
Pre_Processamento: Filtering (50 Hz low pass); EOG artifact detection/elimination; Manual/Automated artifact rejection (absolute voltages > 100 $\mu$V); EOG regression/correction (multivariate linear regression with wavelet-denoised EOG); Epoching
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (at Fz); Alpha (at Pz); 0-64 Hz (for power spectra estimation)
Software_Analise: Neuroscan Scan 4.2 and Scan 4.2 EditTM software
Abordagem_Estatistica: Doubly/Singly multivariate Repeated Measures ANOVA; Within-subjects contrasts; Huynh-Feldt correction
Classificacao_ML: KPLS-DLR (Kernel Partial Least Squares Decomposition with Discrete-output Linear Regression); KPLS-SVC (Support Vector Classifier) tested; Five-fold cross-validation; Test set accuracies

Titulo: Multi-aspect data analysis for investigating human computation mechanism
Autores: Motomura, Shinichi and Zhong, Ning
Amostra_Info: EEG: 10 subjects; fMRI: 11 subjects; Age: - (Not specified); Markers: Healthy subjects (Implied, no diagnosis mentioned)
Processos_Matematicos: Human computation mechanism; Mental arithmetic/Addition problem
Comparacao_Tarefa: Compared to Off-task (looking at random numbers) and No-task (relaxed state)
Posicao_Eletrodos: Fz, Oz, C5, CPz, AF3, CP3 (used for specific analyses); Extended International 10-20 system
Equipamento: BrainAmp amplifier (Brain Products, EEG); 3T Siemens system (fMRI); Brain Vision Analyzer (EEG analysis); Statistical Parametric Mapping (SPM2) (fMRI analysis)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 128 channels; Extended international 10-20 system; Reference: - (Not specified)
Pre_Processamento: Ocular correction; Filter (BPF: 0.5-80 Hz); Baseline correction; Artifact rejection; Slice timing correction; Realignment; Normalization; Smoothing (FWHM=10)
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta (Sub-Delta), Theta, Alpha, Beta
Software_Analise: Brain Vision Analyzer (EEG analysis); Statistical Parametric Mapping (SPM2) (fMRI analysis)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Averaging/ERP derivation; Statistical Thresholding (uncorrected voxel-level intensity threshold of p<0.01 for fMRI)

Titulo: Multi-channel EEG Signal Analysis for Mental Arithmetic Task Classification using Hybrid CNN- LSTM Model
Autores: G. Sirigudi and H. K. Goru and N. K. Reddy Gadi and S. M. Penumaka
Revista: 2025 3rd International Conference on Inventive Computing and Informatics (ICICI)
Amostra_Info: 36 subjects; Age not specified
Processos_Matematicos: Mental Arithmetic Task (MAT)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Before Task condition (Baseline/Rest)
Posicao_Eletrodos: International 10/20 system; 23 channels
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: Silver or silver chloride electrodes; 23 channels; Reference to ear electrodes
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass Butterworth filter of order 2 (0.5 Hz high-pass, 45 Hz low-pass); 50 Hz notch filter; Z-score normalization; Referencing to ear electrodes; Epoching (non-overlapping chunks, 35s window size)
Classificacao_ML: Hybrid CNN-LSTM model; Validation: 80% training, 10% validation, 10% testing split; Metrics: Accuracy, Sensitivity, Specificity, Precision, MCC, Kappa, F1-score

Titulo: Neural Correlates of Mathematical Problem Solving
Autores: Lin, Chun-Ling and Jung, Melody and Wu, Ying Choon and She, Hsiao-Ching and Jung, Tzyy-Ping
Amostra_Info: 11 healthy adults (9 males, 2 females); 15-49 years
Processos_Matematicos: Mathematical problem solving; Mental arithmetic (combining four single-digit numbers to equal 24 - Math24 puzzle)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Pre-stimulus baseline period (2 s)
Variaveis_Fisiologicas: EOG (removed via ICA for blinks, eye moment); EMG (removed via ICA for muscle activity); ECG (removed via ICA for EKG)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Solution latencies (SLs); Accuracy (correctly solved trials)
Posicao_Eletrodos: 12 representative sites: Frontal (F3, F4, Fz), Central (C3, C4, Cz), Parietal (P3, P4, Pz), Occipital (O3, O4, Oz); International 10-20 system (implied)
Equipamento: BioSemi ActiveTwo EEG system; 3D digitizer (Zebris Medical GmbH); MATLAB; EEGLAB
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 128 channels; Technology: Conductive gel; Reference: CMS-DRL ground, re-referenced offline to an average of the left and right mastoids
Pre_Processamento: Notch filter (60 Hz); Band-pass filter (2-30 Hz); Down-sampling (to 256 Hz); Re-referencing (avg mastoids); Visual inspection/Rejection of channels/segments; ICA (to remove blinks, EKG, muscle activity); Epoching; Baseline subtraction; Linear time-warping
Faixas_Frequencia: $\theta$ (4-7 Hz); $\alpha$ (8-13 Hz); $\beta$ (14-30 Hz) (General analysis range 2 to 30 Hz)
Software_Analise: MATLAB (The Mathworks, Inc.); EEGLAB (open source toolbox)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Repeated-measures one-way ANOVA (behavioral); Paired-sample repeated-measure ANOVA (topography); Nonparametric bootstrap method (ERSP reliability)

Titulo: Neuro-cognitive performance is enhanced during short periods of microgravity
Autores: Wollseiffen, Petra and Vogt, Tobias and Abeln, Vera and Strüder, Heiko K. and Askew, Christopher D. and Schneider, Stefan
Amostra_Info: Total 43 participants (female: $n=13$, age $35.0 \pm 8.7$; male: $n=31$, age $39.9 \pm 9.2$ years). 34 for ERP analysis
Marcadores_Amostra: Healthy participants undergoing Parabolic Flight; Subgroups by Flight Experience (First-time vs Frequent Flyers) and Medication Use (Scopolamine)
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic task (executive function/problem solving)
Comparacao_Tarefa: Normal gravity phase ($1G$) during the same parabola
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Reaction time; Accuracy (percentage of errors)
Posicao_Eletrodos: Centro-frontal, Centro-parietal, Superior frontal gyrus, Medial frontal gyrus (BA 6); International 10-20 system
Equipamento: actiCAP (Brain Products GmbH); Brain Vision amplifier and RecView software; Brain Vision Analyzer 2.0
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 32 Ag-AgCl electrodes; Technology: Electro-Gel™; Reference: Electrode mounted in the triangle of FP1, FP2 and Fz
Pre_Processamento: Filters (5-30.0 Hz); Automatic artifact rejection; Baseline correction; Current Source Density (CSD) maps; sLORETA
Faixas_Frequencia: Filter range: 5-30.0 Hz; ERP components N1 and P2 analyzed
Software_Analise: Brain Vision Analyzer 2.0; STATISTICA version 7.1; sLORETA software
Abordagem_Estatistica: Two-way repeated measures ANOVA; Fisher's LSD test; Paired sampled t-test (sLORETA)

Titulo: Neurophysiological evidence for the validity of verbal strategy reports in mental arithmetic
Autores: Grabner, Roland H. and De Smedt, Bert
Amostra_Info: 19 participants (10 males, 9 females); Ages 20 to 31 years (M=24.47, SD = 2.93 years)
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic
Comparacao_Tarefa: Pre-stimulus reference interval (for ERS/ERD computation)
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy (correctly solved trials); Response latency (time from problem onset to speech onset); Verbal strategy reports (Retrieval, Procedure, Other)
Posicao_Eletrodos: 64 scalp electrodes (Extended 10-20 system); Aggregated over 8 cortical areas per Hemisphere (anteriofrontal, frontal, frontocentral, central, centroparietal, parietal, parietooccipital, temporal)
Equipamento: BioSemi Active Two system (EEG); Voice-activated microphone (voice-key)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 scalp electrodes (Active electrodes); Layout: Extended 10-20 system; Reference: Not explicitly stated
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtered (0.5-45 Hz); EOG artifacts reduced by regression method; Visual inspection for artifacts; 3D spline surface Laplacian estimation (spatial filtering)
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (3-6 Hz); Lower alpha (8-10 Hz); Upper alpha (10-13 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Repeated measures ANOVAs (Factors: Strategy, Problem Size, Hemisphere, Area); Huynh-Feldt procedure (correction for sphericity violation); Post hoc comparisons

Titulo: Oscillatory EEG correlates of arithmetic strategy use in addition and subtraction
Autores: De Smedt, Bert and Grabner, Roland H. and Studer, Bettina
Amostra_Info: 20 adults (11 males, 9 females). Mean age = 22.60, SD=3.10.
Processos_Matematicos: Arithmetic strategy use in addition and subtraction, specifically retrieval versus procedural strategies.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Error rates and Reaction times (RT) (measured via a voice-activated device/voice-key).
Posicao_Eletrodos: 64 scalp electrodes according to the extended 10-20 system. Topographically aggregated into eight cortical areas per hemisphere: anteriofrontal (AF), frontal (F), frontocentral (FC), central (C), centroparietal (CP), parietal (P), parietooccipital (PO), and temporal (T).
Equipamento: BioSemi ActiveTwo system (BioSemi, Amsterdam, The Netherlands)
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 64 scalp electrodes. BioSemi ActiveTwo system.
Pre_Processamento: Band-pass filtered between 0.5 and 45 Hz. EOG artefact reduction by a regression method. Visual inspection for artefacts. 3D spline surface Laplacian estimation applied. Reference interval (R) for ERS/ERD: 500 to 2,500 ms after trial onset. Activation interval (A) for ERS/ERD: from problem presentation until 125 ms before oral response.
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (4-7 Hz). Alpha (8-12 Hz).
Abordagem_Estatistica: Repeated measures ANOVA. Huynh-Feldt procedure for correction of degrees of freedom. Bonferroni corrected post hoc t tests.

Titulo: QEEG coherence patterns related to mathematics ability in children
Autores: nzalone, Christopher and Luedke, Jessica C. and Green, Jessica J. and Decker, Scott L.
Amostra_Info: 60 school-aged children. Mean = 9.58 years, SD = 1.38 years.
Marcadores_Amostra: children
Processos_Matematicos: General math skills and broad math abilities. Specifically, the analysis used scores from the Math Calculation, Applied Problems, and Math Fluency subtests.
Comparacao_Tarefa: rest (while the participants were awake, with eyes closed).
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Standardized math achievement scores (from Math Calculation, Applied Problems, and Math Fluency subtests of the WJ-III Ach). Broad Reading composite score (for discriminant validity). Measures of drowsiness (EEG patterns consistent with drowsiness were flagged and removed during artifact processing).
Posicao_Eletrodos: System: 10/20 placement system. Electrodes: 19 channel electrodes. Areas of Interest: Coherence values were converted to a format yielding standardized coherence between each of the 52 Brodmann areas (BAs). The analysis focused on intrahemispheric coherence between Brodmann area 40 (BA 40) and all other BAs.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 channel electrodes.
Pre_Processamento: Filtering: 60 Hz notch filter. Bandwidth range set to 1.0 to 30 Hz. Artifact Removal: Manual visual inspection to remove abnormal amplitude spikes (related to muscle movement). Automated Neuroguide program to identify/flag artifacts related to drowsiness and eye muscle movement (followed by manual review). Automated Neuroguide program used to select and discard all data portions with artifacts, yielding an entirely artifact-free EEG sample.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta: 1.0–4.0 Hz Theta: 4.0–8.0 Hz Alpha 1: 8.0–10.0 Hz Alpha 2: 10.0–12.0 Hz Beta 1: 12.0–15.0 Hz Beta 2: 15.0–18.0 Hz Beta 3: 18.0–25.0 Hz High-beta: 25.0–30.0 Hz
Software_Analise: Programming Language: MATLAB (2018). Analysis Software: Neuroguide program 6.6.4, IBM SPSS (version 24).
Abordagem_Estatistica: Principle Component Analyses (PCA) with varimax rotation. Bivariate Pearson's correlation coefficients. Multiple regression analyses. Selection criteria for regression: Component must correlate significantly with at least two of the three WJ-III Ach math subtests ($p < .05$).
Classificacao_ML: Multiple linear regression was used to create a model that predicts Broad Math composite score and Broad Reading scores.

Titulo: Resolving Gender Difference in Problem Solving Based On the Analysis of Electroencephalogram (EEG) Signals
Autores: Safri, Norlaili Mat and Sha'ameri, Ahmad Zuri and Samah, Narina A. and Daliman, Shaparas
Amostra_Info: 29 healthy undergraduates (16 female and 13 male subjects)
Marcadores_Amostra: Gender
Processos_Matematicos: Problem solving, specifically: mental arithmetic task (continuous subtraction of 7 from 1000) and Tower of Hanoi (TOH) task.
Comparacao_Tarefa: Control task where the subject was at rest with eyes open but empty minded.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Performance statistics (tabulated answer): Abilities in answering arithmetic questions with accurate final value and abilities to complete the TOH task within the maximum time.
Posicao_Eletrodos: Brain regions corresponding to the 10/30 electrode placement system: Frontal (F), Temporal (T), Central (C), Parietal (P), and Occipital (O). Analysis focused on signal sources, predominantly the Frontal (F) regions.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 electrodes based on the 10/30 electrode placement system.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta (0-4 Hz), Theta (4-8 Hz), Alpha (8-13 Hz), Beta (13-31 Hz), Gamma (31-51 Hz), and High gamma (51-120 Hz).
Software_Analise: C-program running on a Linux platform for PDC and PSE output, and Microsoft Excel for t-test.
Abordagem_Estatistica: Calculated statistics of high vs. low performance and t-test.

Titulo: Simple and difficult mathematics in children: A minimum spanning tree EEG network analysis
Autores: Vourkas, Michael and Karakonstantaki, Eleni and Simos, Panagiotis G. and Tsirka, Vasso and Antonakakis, Marios and Vamvoukas, Michael and Stam, Cornelis and Dimitriadis, Stavros and Micheloyannis, Sifis
Amostra_Info: 51 students (MD: n=20, NI: n=31). Screened at Grade 3, with EEG recordings taken one year later when students were in Grade 5 or 6.
Marcadores_Amostra: Children with Math Difficulties (MD) and Typically Achieving Controls (NI)
Processos_Matematicos: Arithmetic tasks, specifically simple addition and subtraction.
Comparacao_Tarefa: Visual fixation (control condition).
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Child Behavior Checklist-Parent Version (CBCL) was administered to assess externalizing and internalizing behavior problems.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy and speed measures on psychometric math tasks (GMST and NUCALC). Response time is implied by the "speed" measure.
Posicao_Eletrodos: 29 electrode sites according to a modified international 10/20 system. Analysis areas included Hemisphere (left, right), Area (fronto-central, temporo-parietal), and specific electrodes (F3/4, F7/8, FC3/4, FT7/8, T3/4, TP7/8, CP3/4, P3/4).
Equipamento: Electrocap, CONTACT Precision Instruments amplifiers, and a DATAQ AD Converter.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 29 electrode sites. System: Modified international 10/20 system. Electrode impedances were kept below 5 KΩ.
Pre_Processamento: High-pass at 0.004 Hz and low-pass at 200 Hz. Artifacts: Online monitoring to select artifact-free periods and offline selection of a single 8s artifact-free epoch per participant and condition.
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (4-6.5 Hz), Alpha1 (6.5-9 Hz), Alpha2 (9-12 Hz), and Beta (12-30 Hz).
Abordagem_Estatistica: Separate ANOVAs (Group as between-subjects, Task as repeated measures factor) and Pearson correlation coefficients.

Titulo: Spectra-temporal patterns underlying mental addition: An ERP and ERD/ERS study
Autores: Ku, Yixuan and Hong, Bo and Gao, Xiaorong and Gao, Shangkai
Amostra_Info: 15 right-handed college students (11 males, 4 females), aged 21–27 years (mean ± SD = 23.6 ± 1.7 years).
Processos_Matematicos: Mental addition.
Comparacao_Tarefa: Control tasks where subjects watched or listened to letters (V-L and A-L).
Variaveis_Fisiologicas: EOG (Electro-oculogram) signal to detect horizontal and vertical eye movements.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy rate (AR) and Reaction time (RT).
Posicao_Eletrodos: Standard 10-20 system. Electrodes grouped into nine regions of interest (ROI). Analysis used factors AP (anterior, center, posterior) and LR (left, medial, right). Results highlight the medial frontal areas, central and parietal areas, and the Cz electrode.
Equipamento: EEG recording system: SynAmps2, Compumedics, Ltd Corp, US.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 60 Ag-AgCl scalp electrodes (Quick-Caps, Neuroscan). System: standard 10-20 system. Reference: linked earlobes.
Pre_Processamento: Filtering: 0.1–100 Hz band-pass. EOG contamination excluded by linear regression in Neuroscan EDIT software. Artifact rejection: Trials with EEG voltages two standard deviations away from the mean were excluded. Epoching: 100 ms before to 1000 ms after every stimulus onset for ERP analysis. Baseline Correction: ERP baseline established from 100 ms preceding the stimulus and subtracted.
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta (4–8 Hz), Alpha (8–13 Hz), and Beta (13–26 Hz).
Software_Analise: Software: Matlab 7.0 (Math Works, US), Neuroscan EDIT software (Compumedics, US), Statistica (StatSoft, US). Toolbox: EEGLAB 6.0 toolbox (Swartz Center for Computational Neurosciences, US).
Abordagem_Estatistica: Repeated measures analysis of variance (ANOVA) (Two-way, Four-way, and Five-way depending on the variable). Post hoc Tukey HSD test. Point-by-point analysis of variance.

Titulo: Stress Identification during Sustained Mental Task and Brain Relaxation Modeling with ߚ ߙ⁄ Band Power Ratio
Autores: Yusuf, Md. Salah Uddin and Islam, Monira and Roy, Shidhartho
Revista: 2019 4th International Conference on Electrical Information and Communication Technology, EICT 2019
Amostra_Info: 52 subjects, aged 20 to 23 (average 22.3)
Processos_Matematicos: Mathematical problem-solving
Comparacao_Tarefa: Relax State / Resting baseline
Equipamento: BIOPAC MP36 system, BIOPAC electrode lead set (SS2L), disposable vinyl electrodes (EL503).
Pre_Processamento: Filtering to remove power line carriers. Raw EEG divided into frequency bands.
Faixas_Frequencia: Alpha (8-13 Hz), Beta (14-30 Hz), Delta (0.5-4 Hz), Theta (4-8 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Power Spectral Density (PSD) analysis. Least square algorithm.
Classificacao_ML: Mathematical modeling (Quadratic expression using least square algorithm)

Titulo: Studying Functional Brain Networks to Understand Mathematical Thinking: A Graph-Theoretical Approach
Autores: Bamparopoulos, Georgios and Klados, Manousos A. and Papathanasiou, Nikolaos and Antoniou, Ioannis and Micheloyannis, Sifis and Bamidis, Panagiotis D.
Amostra_Info: 10 subjects; Ten right-handed medical students of the University of Crete. Age not provided.
Processos_Matematicos: Simple Addition (single-digit numbers) and Difficult Multiplication (two-digit multiplications).
Comparacao_Tarefa: Number Looking (control situation).
Variaveis_Fisiologicas: Ocular artifacts corrected using the REGICA plugin, implying EOG was measured or accounted for.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Accuracy (only pieces "with correct answers" were chosen for analysis).
Posicao_Eletrodos: The nodes of the weighted graphs were the EEG channels. The specific number of channels or system (e.g., 10-20) is not provided.
Abordagem_Estatistica: Kolmogorov-Smirnov test (K-S test); Mann-Whitney U test ($p < 0.05$).

Titulo: Surface EEG shows that functional segregation via phase coupling contributes to the neural substrate of mental calculations
Autores: Dimitriadis, Stavros I. and Kanatsouli, Kassiani and Laskaris, Nikolaos A. and Tsirka, Vasso and Vourkas, Michael and Micheloyannis, Sifis
Amostra_Info: 18 right-handed volunteers;average age of 23 years (age range: 21–26 years).
Marcadores_Amostra: right-handed medical school students
Processos_Matematicos: Multiplication of two-digit numbers and comparison of four-digit numbers.
Comparacao_Tarefa: resting baseline
Variaveis_Comportamentais: accuracy
Posicao_Eletrodos: international 10/20 system, full
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 30 electrodes, plus 2 additional EOG and reference electrodes.
Pre_Processamento: Filtered online with a 0.1–200 Hz bandpass. Segmented into artifact-free epochs of 8 seconds. Independent Component Analysis (ICA) to eliminate artifacts (e.g., eyes, heart, muscles). A zero-phase pass-band filter.Features extracted: Coherence (COH), Nonlinear Interdependence (NI), and Phase Locking Index (PLI).
Faixas_Frequencia: Theta: (4–8 Hz). Alpha 1: (8–10 Hz). Alpha 2:(10–13 Hz). Beta: (13–30 Hz). Gamma:(30–45 Hz).
Abordagem_Estatistica: contrast function (J-index); randomization procedure was used to test the statistical significance of the J-index values.

Titulo: Task Difficulty and EEG Alpha Asymmetry: An Amplitude and Frequency Analysis
Autores: Earle JB
Amostra_Info: 20 right-handed subjects (10 female, 10 male) selected from a liberal-arts college.
Marcadores_Amostra: right-handed with no familial history of left-handedness, and they exhibited more than 50% alpha activity in an eye-closed baseline condition.
Processos_Matematicos: serial subtraction
Comparacao_Tarefa: verbal image-matching task and a spatial part-to-whole matching task. The difficulty levels of the tasks are also compared to each other.
Variaveis_Psicologicas: Math Anxiety Rating Scale (MARS), Verbalizer-Visualizer Questionnaire, Fear of Negative Evaluation Scale.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: accuracy, response time, self-reported ratings (anxiety, confusion, difficulty)
Posicao_Eletrodos: parietal, temporal
Equipamento: Med Associates AN100 preamplifier/physiological amplifiers with an ECI Electro-cap.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 4 recording electrodes (P3, P4, T5, T6), plus one reference electrode (Cz) and EOG electrodes.
Pre_Processamento: filtered through a 0.4–40 Hz passband filter. Alpha activity (8–13 Hz) was isolated with a 4-pole Butterworth alpha filter. Epochs with eye movement artifacts were eliminated. Features extracted: Integrated alpha amplitude. Mean alpha frequency. Alpha amplitude and frequency asymmetry ratios
Faixas_Frequencia: Alpha : (8-13Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA, Across-subject correlational analysis, Median splits.

Titulo: Task-Independent Cognitive Workload Discrimination Based on EEG with Stacked Graph Attention Convolutional Networks
Autores: Chenyu Wei, Xuewen Zhao,Yu Song and Yi Liu
Amostra_Info: 15 graduate students (11 male, 4 female) with a mean age of 24 years old (aged 22 to 25).
Marcadores_Amostra: graduate students, native Chinese speakers, right-handed, and have normal or corrected-to-normal vision.
Processos_Matematicos: mental arithmetic task, which involves addition operations with single-digit, two-digit, and three-digit numbers
Comparacao_Tarefa: working memory task (N-back) and a short-term memory task (Sternberg). The different workload levels within the mental arithmetic task are also compared to each other.
Variaveis_Comportamentais: self-reported measure of cognitive workload.
Posicao_Eletrodos: international 10/20 system, full
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 59-channel
Pre_Processamento: Down-sampled from 1000 Hz to 200 Hz. Bandpass filters (1–75 Hz) and a trap filter (49–51 Hz). A global averaging reference was applied. Independent Component Analysis (ICA), The signals were categorized into four frequency bands using a Butterworth filter. Features extracted: node information: Differential Entropy (DE) and Power Spectral Density (PSD) for each channel. Network structure: Phase-Locked Values (PLV), Phase-Lag Indices (PLI), Pearson Correlation Coefficients (PCC), and Mutual Information (MI) were used to generate the functional brain network.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta (1–3 Hz) Theta (4–8 Hz) Alpha (8–12 Hz) Beta (12–30 Hz)
Abordagem_Estatistica: ANOVA, cross-validation
Classificacao_ML: Stacked Graph Attention Convolutional Networks (SGATCNs) model, along with comparative models including GAT, GCN, SVM, RF, KNN, and LR.

Titulo: The analysis of EEG coherence reflects middle childhood differences in mathematical achievement
Autores: González-Garrido, Andrés A. and Gómez-Velázquez, Fabiola R. and Salido-Ruiz, Ricardo A. and Espinoza-Valdez, Aurora and Vélez-Pérez, Hugo and Romo-Vazquez, Rebeca and Gallardo-Moreno, Geisa B. and Ruiz-Stovel, Vanessa D. and Martínez-Ramos, Alicia and Berumen, Gustavo
Amostra_Info: 40 children, all aged 8-to-9 years old. High Achievement (HA) Group: 20 children (6 females), mean age 9.1 years. Low Achievement (LA) Group: 20 children (10 females), mean age 9.1 years.
Marcadores_Amostra: math achievement scores on the WRAT-4, right-handed, IQ of 90 or higher
Variaveis_Comportamentais: accuracy, reaction times, non-responses
Posicao_Eletrodos: full
Equipamento: Electro-Cap with a MEDICID-04 system (Neuronic Systems, Inc.).
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 19 scalp electrode site
Pre_Processamento: filtered at a band-pass of 0.5-to-30 Hz. Blind Source Separation. Features extracted: Magnitude-squared coherence. Time-frequency domain analysis of spectral power.
Faixas_Frequencia: Delta, Theta Alpha, Beta. The full range was 0-to-30 Hz.
Abordagem_Estatistica: mixed-design, repeated measures ANOVA, one-way ANOVA with Tukey’s honest significant difference (HSD) corrections

Titulo: Towards Predicting Attention and Workload During Math Problem Solving
Autores: Tato, Ange and Nkambou, Roger and Ghali, Ramla
Revista: Lecture Notes in Computer Science (including subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics)
Amostra_Info: 17 alunos (10 F, 7 M). Idade: entre 9 e 11 anos (M=10,05; DP=0,42).
Processos_Matematicos: Internal comparison to past mental state values/events
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Attention And Workload
Posicao_Eletrodos: CenterLeft and CenterRight brain hemisphere measures (Left/Right data excluded from final model)
Equipamento: Neeuro Senzeband non-invasive EEG headset; Netmath web application
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 4 raw EEG channels
Pre_Processamento: Epoching (20s timestep); Attempted standardization and normalization (raw values retained)
Abordagem_Estatistica: Binary cross entropy (loss); Adam (optimizer)
Classificacao_ML: LSTM

Titulo: Training Machine Learning Models to Detect Group Differences in Neurophysiological Data using Recurrence Quantification Analysis based Features
Autores: Guglielmo, Gianluca and Wiltshire, Travis J. and Louwerse, Max
Revista: International Conference on Agents and Artificial Intelligence
Amostra_Info: 36 healthy participants; Age not specified
Processos_Matematicos: Mental arithmetic/Numerical skills
Variaveis_Comportamentais: Calculations per minute
Posicao_Eletrodos: F7, Pz, P4, and Fp1. Frontal and parietal regions. International 10/20 scheme.
Equipamento: EEG 23-channel system. No specific brand/model mentioned.
Tecnologia_Eletrodos: 23 EEG channels. Layout: International 10/20 scheme. Technology/Reference: Not specified.
Pre_Processamento: Filters: Low pass filter (45 Hz) and a power notch filter (50 Hz). Data is reported as artifact-free.
Software_Analise: Python (Pyunicorn library). R (Tserieschaos package, nonlinearTseries package).
Abordagem_Estatistica: Randomized Search (for hyperparameter selection); Extra Trees method (for feature selection); 5-fold cross-validation.
Classificacao_ML: Classifiers: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Gradient Boosting Classifier (GBC). Technique: SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique) for class balancing. Validation: 5-fold cross-validation. Metrics: Accuracy.
